name: ABeeZee
classification_notes: ABeeZee features open, rounded letterforms designed specifically for early readers, with simplified construction and generous x-height. The forms are clear and unambiguous, prioritising legibility for children.
summary: A child-focused rounded sans designed to support early literacy with clear, open letterforms.
description: ABeeZee was created with beginning readers in mind, using simplified letter shapes that avoid ambiguity between similar characters. Its rounded strokes and open apertures make it particularly well-suited to educational materials, children's books, and primary school interfaces. It functions best at larger sizes where its friendly character is most apparent.

name: ADLaM Display
classification_notes: ADLaM Display is designed primarily to support the Fula language using the ADLaM script, a relatively new alphabet developed in the late 1980s. The Latin companion follows a display serif style with moderate contrast.
summary: A display typeface built around the ADLaM script, bringing typographic support to the Fula-speaking world.
description: ADLaM Display is one of very few typefaces to support the ADLaM script, which was created by Ibrahima and Abdoulaye Barry to write the Fula language. Its display orientation makes it best suited to headlines, titles, and cultural materials rather than extended body text. The font represents an important step in digital inclusion for West African languages.

name: AR One Sans
classification_notes: AR One Sans is engineered for legibility in augmented reality and screen environments, with features that resist irradiation and halation at small sizes. Its letterforms are subtly modified to maintain clarity under adverse rendering conditions.
summary: A technically motivated humanist sans built specifically to remain legible in augmented reality and challenging screen contexts.
description: AR One Sans addresses the practical problem of text rendered over unpredictable backgrounds in AR interfaces, incorporating design adjustments that counteract optical blur and low contrast conditions. Its multiple weights give designers enough range for clear typographic hierarchy in UI-driven environments. It also covers Vietnamese alongside standard Latin, broadening its utility across multilingual products.

name: Abel
classification_notes: Abel is a narrow, upright sans-serif with a condensed structure influenced by early twentieth-century gothics, giving it a utilitarian yet characterful appearance. Its single weight limits versatility but the tight proportions suit headline use well.
summary: A clean condensed sans with early gothic roots, well-suited to headlines and space-constrained settings.
description: Abel draws on the tradition of condensed gothic lettering to produce a typeface that feels both functional and slightly retro without being overtly decorative. Its single Regular weight keeps it focused, making it a reliable choice for newspaper-style headlines, poster typography, and web headers where horizontal space is limited. It lacks the weight range needed for full typographic systems but excels as a display companion.

name: Abhaya Libre
classification_notes: Abhaya Libre is a serious text serif supporting the Sinhala script alongside Latin, designed with humanist proportions and moderate stroke contrast. The Sinhala letterforms are crafted to harmonise with the Latin in weight and rhythm.
summary: A multilingual humanist serif offering robust support for Sinhala and Latin in a single cohesive text family.
description: Abhaya Libre fills a genuine gap in quality Sinhala typography, providing a professionally designed typeface suitable for books, documents, and editorial work in Sri Lankan and South Asian publishing contexts. Its five-weight range from Regular to ExtraBold makes it genuinely versatile for typographic hierarchy. The Latin component is a competent humanist serif that pairs naturally with the Sinhala, making bilingual layouts coherent.

name: Aboreto
classification_notes: Aboreto is an all-caps display typeface featuring tall, elegant letterforms with delicate hairline details and decorative flourishes that evoke high-end fashion and luxury branding. Its extreme weight contrast and refined construction limit it firmly to display use.
summary: An all-caps luxury display face with extreme hairline contrast and decorative detailing suited to fashion and prestige branding.
description: Aboreto occupies a narrow but well-defined niche, delivering the refined, high-contrast aesthetic associated with couture fashion houses and premium packaging. Its single weight and all-caps nature make it a deliberately focused tool rather than a general-purpose workhorse. It is best deployed sparingly — for logotypes, masthead treatments, and short headline copy where its elegance can read without compromise.

name: Abril Fatface
classification_notes: Abril Fatface is a bold display serif modelled on nineteenth-century fat face types, featuring extreme stroke contrast with hairline serifs set against very heavy strokes. It is expressly designed for headline impact at large sizes.
summary: A commanding fat face display serif that channels nineteenth-century poster typography with extreme stroke contrast.
description: Abril Fatface belongs to the fat face tradition of British and Continental display types, where the interplay between ultra-bold strokes and near-invisible hairlines creates immediate visual drama. Designed by TypeTogether, it is best used for large-scale headline work, magazine covers, posters, and branding applications where typographic personality is the point. It pairs naturally with the broader Abril family for editorial contexts requiring both display and text weights.

name: Abyssinica SIL
classification_notes: Abyssinica SIL is a Unicode-compliant serif designed specifically for the Ethiopic script, developed by SIL International to support the many languages written in Ge'ez-derived writing systems. The design prioritises legibility and scholarly accuracy over stylistic flourish.
summary: A Unicode-compliant Ethiopic serif from SIL International, providing reliable text support for Ge'ez-based writing systems.
description: Abyssinica SIL was developed as part of SIL International's broader commitment to linguistic inclusion, covering a large range of Ethiopic characters used across Amharic, Tigrinya, and related languages. Its design is scholarly and functional rather than decorative, making it appropriate for academic publishing, Bible translation, and documentation projects. For anyone working with Ethiopic scripts, it remains one of the most comprehensive and reliable free options available.

name: Aclonica
classification_notes: Aclonica features bold, chunky letterforms with a slight retro flavour and strong personality. Its thick strokes and distinctive terminals make it unsuitable for body text but effective at large sizes.
summary: A bold, characterful display sans with retro sensibility and strong visual weight.
description: Aclonica's thick, confident strokes and quirky detailing give it a vintage American feel that suits headline and poster work. It performs best at large sizes where its personality can land fully. Use it for branding, packaging, or editorial headlines that need attitude.

name: Acme
classification_notes: Acme is a compact, upright display sans with slightly condensed proportions and a clean, punchy character. Its letterforms are optimised for headlines rather than extended reading.
summary: A tight, punchy display sans built for short, impactful headlines.
description: Acme's condensed proportions and clean construction make it well suited to headlines, posters, and digital banners where space is at a premium. Its single weight limits versatility but the design is confident and legible at large sizes. It works particularly well for sports, gaming, or youth-oriented branding.

name: Actor
classification_notes: Actor is a lightweight humanist sans with open, airy letterforms and a restrained, neutral character. Its thin strokes and generous spacing lend it a quiet elegance at body sizes.
summary: A quiet, lightweight humanist sans that reads cleanly at small sizes without drawing attention to itself.
description: Actor's open apertures and even stroke weight make it a reliable choice for UI text, editorial body copy, and digital interfaces where legibility is paramount. Its single weight is a notable limitation for hierarchical typesetting. The overall feel is understated and professional rather than distinctive.

name: Adamina
classification_notes: Adamina is a sturdy transitional serif with moderate contrast, robust serifs, and ink-trap-inspired details that aid legibility in print and on screen. Its design references classic book typography.
summary: A sturdy transitional serif with ink-trap details designed for comfortable, reliable body text.
description: Adamina draws on the tradition of transitional book faces, offering moderate stroke contrast and well-proportioned letterforms that sustain long reading. It suits editorial work, longform web articles, and digital books where readability over extended passages is essential. Its single weight limits its use in complex typographic hierarchies.

name: Advent Pro
classification_notes: Advent Pro is a clean geometric sans with a wide weight range and notably condensed proportions that give it a modern, technical edge. Its multi-script support across Latin, Cyrillic, and Greek adds considerable utility.
summary: A clean geometric sans with condensed proportions, a broad weight range, and strong multilingual support.
description: Advent Pro's narrow letterforms and geometric construction make it effective for space-efficient interfaces, dashboards, and technical applications. Its full weight range from Thin to Black provides genuine typographic flexibility. Support for Cyrillic Extended and Greek makes it a practical choice for multilingual European projects.

name: Afacad
classification_notes: Afacad is a contemporary humanist sans with open apertures, friendly proportions, and a balanced weight range suited to both UI and editorial work. Its Cyrillic Extended support broadens its practical reach.
summary: A contemporary humanist sans with friendly proportions and solid multilingual coverage for UI and editorial use.
description: Afacad sits comfortably between neutral system sans and expressive humanist, making it versatile enough for both interface design and print body text. Its four weights cover everyday typographic needs without excess. Cyrillic Extended support makes it a dependable option for Eastern European digital projects.

name: Afacad Flux
classification_notes: Afacad Flux is a variable-font extension of the Afacad family, offering a full weight axis from Thin to Black for fine-grained typographic control. It shares the parent family's humanist warmth while gaining expressive range.
summary: A variable humanist sans offering the full weight spectrum of the Afacad family for maximum typographic flexibility.
description: Afacad Flux leverages variable font technology to provide smooth interpolation across nine weights, making it ideal for responsive design systems and performance-conscious web projects. Its humanist letterforms remain legible and approachable at all weights from Thin to Black. The Latin Extended and Vietnamese script support adds useful multilingual capability.

name: Agbalumo
classification_notes: Agbalumo is an expressive display typeface with roots in West African writing traditions, featuring lively, irregular letterforms and broad script support including Ethiopic and Cyrillic Extended. Its decorative character is better suited to display than text use.
summary: An expressive, culturally distinctive display face drawing on West African design traditions with unusually broad script support.
description: Agbalumo stands out on Google Fonts for its celebration of African typographic heritage, incorporating Ethiopic script alongside Latin and Cyrillic Extended in a single expressive typeface. Its organic, lively letterforms suit poster work, cultural branding, and editorial headlines that call for something beyond the conventional. It is firmly a display face and should not be used for body text.

name: Agdasima
classification_notes: A narrow, condensed sans-serif with a utilitarian character and modest stroke contrast. Designed for tight settings where horizontal space is at a premium.
summary: A no-frills condensed sans built for compact headlines and space-efficient typesetting.
description: Agdasima offers a stripped-back condensed form that works well in headlines, banners, and any context demanding maximum text in minimal width. Its clean geometry gives it a functional, workmanlike quality. It suits editorial layouts, packaging, and digital interfaces where brevity of space is a constraint.

name: Agu Display
classification_notes: A heavy display typeface with bold, expressive strokes and a hand-crafted quality. Designed primarily for large-scale decorative use rather than running text.
summary: A bold, characterful display face built for eye-catching headlines and poster work.
description: Agu Display makes a strong visual statement through its weighty, expressive letterforms, which feel hand-influenced despite their digital construction. It is best reserved for large settings where its personality can register fully. Its Vietnamese script support broadens its utility across multilingual projects.

name: Aguafina Script
classification_notes: A flowing, calligraphic display script with thin, elegant strokes and generous swashes. Evokes formal pen lettering suited to decorative applications.
summary: A delicate, calligraphic script that brings an air of elegance to display settings.
description: Aguafina Script draws on formal penmanship traditions, with thin strokes and fluid connections that give it a refined, feminine quality. It works best at large sizes where its fine strokes remain legible and impactful. Packaging, wedding stationery, fashion branding, and editorial accents are its natural home.

name: Akatab
classification_notes: A sturdy humanist sans-serif with notable Tifinagh script support, designed to serve the Amazigh language community alongside Latin. Offers a broad weight range for typographic flexibility.
summary: A practical humanist sans with rare Tifinagh support, making it a genuinely useful tool for multilingual publishing.
description: Akatab stands out primarily for its inclusion of the Tifinagh script, used to write Tamazight and related Amazigh languages, making it an important resource for underserved language communities. Its humanist sans design is clean and readable across a wide range of sizes and weights. It is well suited to body text, UI design, and any project requiring Latin and Tifinagh co-existence.

name: Akaya Kanadaka
classification_notes: A display typeface supporting Kannada and Latin scripts, with rounded, informal letterforms that carry a playful, hand-drawn character. Designed for decorative use rather than extended reading.
summary: A cheerful, rounded display face that bridges Kannada and Latin scripts with consistent playful energy.
description: Akaya Kanadaka provides a rare bilingual display option for designers working with Kannada-language content, maintaining visual harmony between the two scripts. Its rounded, informal strokes give it a friendly, approachable quality well suited to children's publishing, casual branding, and festive materials. It is strictly a display face and should not be used for body text.

name: Akaya Telivigala
classification_notes: A decorative display typeface spanning Telugu and Latin scripts, with rounded forms and an informal hand-lettered quality. Designed for headlines and short decorative passages.
summary: A friendly display face uniting Telugu and Latin scripts in a consistent rounded, informal style.
description: Akaya Telivigala mirrors the approach of its Kannada sibling, delivering a cohesive bilingual display experience for Telugu-language projects. The rounded, hand-influenced letterforms lend warmth and informality, making it a strong candidate for children's content, casual advertising, and cultural materials. Like all single-weight display faces, it works best at large sizes in short bursts.

name: Akronim
classification_notes: A highly stylised display typeface featuring dramatic inline and outline effects, designed to evoke early-twentieth-century titling and signage lettering. Extremely limited in practical application.
summary: A novelty display face with strong retro titling character, best used sparingly for period-appropriate decorative work.
description: Akronim draws directly from early-twentieth-century display and signage lettering traditions, employing heavy inline detailing that gives each letter a crafted, typographic poster quality. Its usefulness is narrow by design — it is a statement face for headlines, short titles, and thematic decoration where period atmosphere is the goal. Overuse will quickly exhaust its charm.

name: Akshar
classification_notes: A clean humanist sans-serif with Devanagari and Latin support, offering a carefully considered weight range for versatile use in bilingual typesetting. The Devanagari design shows attention to script-appropriate rhythm and proportion.
summary: A reliable humanist sans that handles Devanagari and Latin with equal care across a useful weight range.
description: Akshar was developed with bilingual publishing in mind, providing a consistent visual voice across Devanagari and Latin scripts — a combination that remains underserved in quality typefaces. Its humanist proportions and clean strokes make it comfortable for body text, digital interfaces, and editorial work. The five-weight range gives designers enough flexibility to establish clear typographic hierarchy within a single family.

name: Akt
classification_notes: Akt is a clean, contemporary geometric sans-serif with a wide weight range and strong multilingual support including Cyrillic and Greek. Its forms are rational and precise, suited to corporate and editorial contexts.
summary: A versatile geometric sans with an extensive weight range and broad script coverage across Latin, Cyrillic, and Greek.
description: Akt offers nine weights from Thin to Black, making it a reliable workhorse for multi-platform projects requiring consistency across languages. Its geometric construction keeps it feeling modern and neutral, while the Cyrillic Extended and Greek support broadens its usefulness for international branding or editorial work. A solid choice for interface design, corporate identity, and multilingual publishing.

name: Aladin
classification_notes: Aladin is a decorative display face with flowing, calligraphic qualities and an exotic, handcrafted feel. It is available only in Regular weight, limiting its range to headline and decorative applications.
summary: A whimsical, calligraphic display face with an ornate, hand-lettered character suited to decorative headlines.
description: Aladin draws on flowing script traditions to produce letterforms that feel playful and handcrafted, with a slightly exotic personality. Its single-weight offering restricts it to purely decorative roles such as poster headlines, packaging accents, or event branding. It works best at large sizes where its individual character details can be appreciated.

name: Alan Sans
classification_notes: Alan Sans is a modern geometric sans with a broad weight range from Light to Black, featuring clean, open forms. It has good Latin and Latin Extended coverage, making it practical for contemporary design work.
summary: A clean, modern geometric sans offering a wide weight range for versatile branding and screen use.
description: Alan Sans presents rational geometric construction with open, approachable letterforms across seven weights. The range from Light to Black gives designers flexibility for both body text hierarchies and bold display work. It suits contemporary branding, digital interfaces, and editorial layouts where a clean, unfussy sans is required.

name: Alata
classification_notes: Alata is a single-weight humanist sans with slightly geometric qualities and good Vietnamese language support. Its even stroke weight and open apertures give it a calm, readable quality.
summary: A calm, readable single-weight humanist sans with strong Vietnamese support and clean, open letterforms.
description: Alata balances humanist warmth with geometric discipline, producing a typeface that reads comfortably at text sizes on screen. Its Vietnamese script coverage makes it particularly useful for multilingual digital projects targeting South-East Asian audiences. The single weight limits its typographic hierarchy, but it performs well as a body or UI text face.

name: Alatsi
classification_notes: Alatsi is a single-weight geometric sans with Cyrillic Extended support, featuring clean construction and a contemporary feel. Its minimal weight offering restricts its range but it covers a broad character set.
summary: A clean geometric sans with Cyrillic Extended support, best suited to single-weight digital and branding applications.
description: Alatsi offers a straightforward geometric construction with good Cyrillic Extended coverage, making it useful for projects that span Latin and Slavic language markets. The sole Regular weight keeps it focused on contexts where typographic variation is handled through size and layout rather than weight contrast. It works well in UI, digital branding, and editorial settings where simplicity is valued.

name: Albert Sans
classification_notes: Albert Sans is a contemporary geometric sans with a full weight range from Thin to Black, combining rational structure with subtly humanist details. It is well-suited to modern branding and user interface design.
summary: A refined geometric sans blending rational structure with humanist warmth across a full nine-weight range.
description: Albert Sans achieves a balance between the precision of geometric sans design and the legibility benefits of humanist letterforms, giving it broad appeal across branding, editorial, and digital contexts. The complete weight spectrum from Thin to Black enables rich typographic hierarchies within a single typeface family. It is a strong candidate for brand systems requiring consistency from small UI text to large display headlines.

name: Aldrich
classification_notes: Aldrich is a single-weight sans with angular, industrial characteristics reminiscent of technical lettering and signage traditions. Its distinctive letterforms give it a strong, mechanical personality.
summary: An angular, industrial single-weight sans with a mechanical character suited to technical and display use.
description: Aldrich draws on engineering and signage lettering traditions, with letterforms that feel structured and utilitarian rather than neutral. Its angular details and strong silhouettes make it effective for headlines, logotypes, and contexts where a technical or industrial aesthetic is desired. The single Regular weight limits its flexibility but reinforces its distinct, purposeful personality.

name: Alef
classification_notes: Alef is a two-weight sans designed specifically to support Hebrew alongside Latin, with clean, open letterforms suitable for text setting. Its bilingual design makes it a rare and practical choice for Hebrew-Latin projects.
summary: A bilingual sans designed to harmonise Hebrew and Latin scripts in a single, readable typeface.
description: Alef addresses a genuine need for coherent Hebrew-Latin typography, offering Regular and Bold weights that work compatibly across both scripts. Its clean, open letterforms prioritise legibility at text sizes, making it suitable for digital interfaces, editorial content, and documents requiring both scripts. For projects targeting Hebrew-speaking audiences, it is one of the more considered and practical options available on Google Fonts.

name: Alegreya
classification_notes: Alegreya is a dynamic, literary serif with calligraphic roots, featuring high contrast strokes and lively italics. It draws on Renaissance letterform traditions while remaining highly legible at text sizes.
summary: A spirited literary serif with calligraphic energy, built for long-form reading across multiple scripts.
description: Alegreya brings warmth and rhythm to extended text, making it well suited to books, literary journals, and academic publications. Its expressive italics and broad script support — including Cyrillic and Greek — give it considerable versatility for multilingual editorial projects.

name: Alegreya SC
classification_notes: Alegreya SC is a dedicated small caps companion to Alegreya, sharing its calligraphic character while providing true optical small capitals throughout. The result is a refined typographic tool for formal text settings.
summary: The small caps companion to Alegreya, offering true optical small capitals for refined editorial typography.
description: Alegreya SC provides genuine small caps rather than scaled-down capitals, preserving stroke weight and visual harmony in running text. It is ideal for chapter headings, running headers, and formal typographic hierarchies in literary or academic contexts.

name: Alegreya Sans
classification_notes: Alegreya Sans retains the calligraphic rhythm and stroke contrast of the serif original but strips away the serifs, producing a humanist sans with genuine warmth and movement. Its wide weight range and multi-script coverage make it highly adaptable.
summary: A humanist sans that carries the calligraphic spirit of Alegreya into a clean, versatile sans-serif form.
description: Alegreya Sans pairs naturally with its serif counterpart, making it an excellent choice for mixed-typography editorial layouts. Its stroke modulation and lively proportions distinguish it from more neutral sans options, suiting it to book design, cultural branding, and longform digital reading.

name: Alegreya Sans SC
classification_notes: Alegreya Sans SC mirrors the relationship between Alegreya and Alegreya SC, providing true small capitals for the humanist sans family. It maintains optical consistency and typographic precision across its weight range.
summary: The small caps companion to Alegreya Sans, enabling precise typographic hierarchies in humanist sans settings.
description: Alegreya Sans SC provides optically correct small capitals that hold their weight alongside full capitals without appearing spindly or oversized. It serves formal editorial work well, particularly where headings and running text need subtle differentiation without resorting to size changes alone.

name: Aleo
classification_notes: Aleo is a contemporary slab serif with gently rounded terminals and humanist proportions, designed to work effectively at both display and text sizes. Its extensive weight range and open apertures give it strong screen legibility.
summary: A contemporary slab serif with humanist warmth and rounded details, equally comfortable in body text and headlines.
description: Aleo bridges the gap between the authority of slab serifs and the approachability of humanist design, making it suitable for editorial, branding, and web use. Its open letterforms and generous x-height perform reliably across screen sizes, and its wide weight range — from Thin to Black — provides ample typographic range.

name: Alex Brush
classification_notes: Alex Brush is a flowing, connected formal script with thin, elegant strokes inspired by traditional copperplate calligraphy. It is designed for display use at larger sizes where its delicate letterforms can be fully appreciated.
summary: A delicate, flowing formal script with copperplate elegance suited to refined display applications.
description: Alex Brush works well at display sizes for wedding stationery, luxury packaging, and upmarket branding where a hand-lettered feel is desired. Its thin, continuous strokes demand clean backgrounds and careful sizing — it is not suited to small text or screen-heavy contexts.

name: Alexandria
classification_notes: Alexandria is a clean, contemporary geometric sans with Arabic and Latin support, featuring a wide weight range and neutral, modern proportions. Its dual-script design balances the structural demands of Arabic with the geometric clarity of its Latin counterpart.
summary: A clean geometric sans with strong Arabic and Latin support, built for modern multilingual branding and interfaces.
description: Alexandria's dual-script coverage makes it a practical choice for projects requiring consistent typographic voice across Arabic and Latin content. Its neutral, geometric character suits branding, digital interfaces, and editorial work where clarity and cross-script harmony are priorities.

name: Alfa Slab One
classification_notes: Alfa Slab One is an ultra-bold display slab serif with heavy, rectangular serifs and intense optical weight, designed purely for large-scale headline use. It draws on nineteenth-century wood-type traditions with a confident, block-like presence.
summary: A thunderous display slab with wood-type boldness, built for headlines and posters that demand immediate impact.
description: Alfa Slab One is unapologetically heavy, making it most effective at large sizes where its slab serifs and dense mass create strong visual anchors. It suits posters, sports branding, editorial headlines, and any context calling for raw typographic authority. It has no place in body text or UI settings.

name: Alice
classification_notes: Alice features sturdy, slightly condensed letterforms with moderate contrast and robust serifs, drawing on classic book typography traditions. Its Cyrillic support and readable proportions make it practical for text use.
summary: A sturdy transitional serif with solid Cyrillic support and reliable text-setting qualities.
description: Alice carries a bookish, old-world character through its moderately contrasted strokes and well-proportioned serifs. It performs well in long-form editorial and literary contexts where legibility and a degree of formality are required. The inclusion of Cyrillic and Cyrillic Extended scripts broadens its appeal for multilingual publishing projects.

name: Alien Block
classification_notes: Alien Block mimics pixelated or blocklike alien-script aesthetics with rigid, geometric letterforms designed purely for decorative impact. It has virtually no utility beyond themed display applications.
summary: A novelty display font built around blocky, alien-inspired letterforms with extremely limited practical use.
description: Alien Block leans entirely into its sci-fi, retro-gaming aesthetic, offering rigid geometric shapes that evoke pixelated or extraterrestrial lettering. It is a one-trick typeface suited only to highly specific themed projects such as game interfaces, Halloween graphics, or science-fiction branding. Outside of those narrow contexts it offers little versatility.

name: Alike
classification_notes: Alike presents clean, open letterforms with low-to-moderate contrast and gently bracketed serifs that blend classical structure with a contemporary lightness. Its math script support gives it a niche academic utility.
summary: A clean, unpretentious humanist serif with a light touch and useful support for mathematical typesetting.
description: Alike sits in a comfortable middle ground between classical and modern serif design, with open counters and restrained ornamentation that aid readability on screen. Its inclusion of math scripts makes it a sensible choice for academic or scientific digital publications. The single Regular weight does limit its flexibility for complex typographic hierarchies.

name: Alike Angular
classification_notes: Alike Angular replaces the rounded terminals of its sibling with sharper, more angular cuts, giving the typeface a crisper and more architectural character. This distinction makes it feel more assertive and contemporary.
summary: The angular sibling of Alike, with sharper terminals that lend the design a more architectural, contemporary edge.
description: Alike Angular takes the same foundational structure as Alike but introduces pointed, angular terminals that push the design towards a more modern, almost constructivist sensibility. This makes it better suited to headlines and branding than to sustained body copy. Like its counterpart, it includes math script support, extending its usefulness into technical and academic contexts.

name: Alkalami
classification_notes: Alkalami is designed for Quranic and traditional Arabic text, following Ruq'ah and simplified Nastaliq conventions with carefully crafted diacritical mark spacing. Its Latin companion is minimal and secondary to the Arabic design.
summary: A specialist Arabic serif crafted for traditional and Quranic text, with meticulous attention to diacritical accuracy.
description: Alkalami is a purpose-built typeface for scholarly and religious Arabic publishing, particularly contexts requiring precise rendering of vowel marks and Quranic annotations. Its design draws from classical Arabic calligraphic traditions while remaining practical for digital typesetting. The Latin script support is functional rather than expressive, reinforcing that this is primarily a tool for Arabic language publishing.

name: Alkatra
classification_notes: Alkatra is a display typeface supporting Bengali, Devanagari, and Latin scripts with a unified expressive visual language across all three. Its varying weights give it flexibility for headline and branding use across South Asian and Latin typography.
summary: A multi-script display typeface bridging Bengali, Devanagari, and Latin with a cohesive expressive character.
description: Alkatra is notable for its genuine typographic harmony across three distinct scripts, a difficult achievement that makes it a strong choice for multilingual branding and editorial work in South Asian contexts. Its weight range from Regular to Bold provides sufficient contrast for building typographic hierarchies. The expressive, slightly informal personality suits cultural, entertainment, and lifestyle branding projects.

name: Allan
classification_notes: Allan combines blackletter-inspired stroke contrast and pointed terminals with a more contemporary display structure, giving it a dark, dramatic quality. Its two weights offer basic flexibility for headline use.
summary: A dramatic display face drawing on blackletter traditions to deliver a dark, pointed headline character.
description: Allan takes the high contrast and angular terminals associated with blackletter typography and filters them through a more accessible, contemporary display sensibility. The result is striking enough for posters, editorial headers, and gothic-inflected branding without requiring the reader to fully decode a traditional blackletter. The Bold weight amplifies the drama considerably, making it the more distinctive of the two cuts.

name: Allerta
classification_notes: Allerta is a clean, upright grotesque sans with even stroke weights and open apertures, designed with legibility and screen rendering in mind. Its single weight restricts its typographic versatility.
summary: A plain, functional grotesque sans-serif built for clean screen rendering and everyday digital use.
description: Allerta is an unfussy, neutral grotesque that prioritises legibility over personality, making it a dependable choice for interface text and screen-based reading contexts. Its open letterforms and even strokes hold up well at smaller sizes on digital displays. The availability of only a single Regular weight does limit its usefulness in projects that demand typographic range, though a companion stencil variant exists for those wanting a decorative counterpart.

name: Allerta Stencil
classification_notes: A geometric stencil typeface with clean, angular cuts that simulate stencilled letterforms. The design maintains legibility while retaining the characteristic interrupted strokes of stencil lettering.
summary: A clean geometric stencil face that brings industrial utility to display settings.
description: Allerta Stencil takes the functional aesthetic of stencilled signage and refines it into a consistent, web-ready typeface. The interrupted strokes give it an unmistakable utilitarian character suited to headlines, packaging, and any context requiring a military or industrial edge. Its single weight limits versatility but makes a strong statement at large sizes.

name: Allison
classification_notes: A high-contrast, formal calligraphic script with pronounced thick-thin transitions and elegant connecting strokes. The letterforms are upright and refined, evoking traditional copperplate penmanship.
summary: An elegant high-contrast script that channels formal copperplate tradition into a digital-ready typeface.
description: Allison delivers the refined quality of copperplate engraving with its dramatic thick-thin stroke contrast and graceful connecting letterforms. It suits luxury branding, wedding stationery, editorial headers, and fashion contexts where a sense of occasion is required. Extended Latin and Vietnamese support broadens its practical reach beyond purely decorative use.

name: Allkin
classification_notes: A decorative display typeface with highly stylised, experimental letterforms designed for expressive headline use. The design prioritises visual impact over conventional readability.
summary: A novelty display face built for visual impact rather than sustained reading.
description: Allkin occupies the niche of experimental display typography, offering letterforms that demand attention at large sizes. Its applications are narrow — primarily posters, short headlines, and bold branding moments where personality trumps neutrality. The single weight restricts its flexibility considerably.

name: Allura
classification_notes: A flowing formal script with moderate stroke contrast and smooth, rhythmic connecting letterforms. Less dramatic than copperplate-derived scripts, it balances elegance with approachability.
summary: A graceful formal script that sits comfortably between casual and ceremonial registers.
description: Allura provides smooth, well-proportioned letterforms with enough stroke contrast to feel polished without the severity of strict copperplate models. It works well for wedding stationery, lifestyle branding, logotypes, and editorial accent text. Coverage of Latin Extended and Vietnamese makes it more broadly useful than many comparable scripts.

name: Almarai
classification_notes: A contemporary humanist sans-serif designed specifically for Arabic and Latin bilingual typesetting, with harmonious proportions across both scripts. The Arabic component follows a modern, simplified approach while maintaining warmth and readability.
summary: A thoughtfully designed bilingual typeface that achieves genuine harmony between Arabic and Latin scripts.
description: Almarai was commissioned to serve Arabic-language digital products where both scripts must coexist at consistent weights and optical sizes. The family spans four weights, providing sufficient range for hierarchical typesetting across interfaces, editorial layouts, and branding applications. Its clean, neutral character makes it a reliable workhorse for any Arabic-Latin project.

name: Almendra
classification_notes: A graceful old-style serif with calligraphic roots, moderate stroke contrast, and slightly bracketed serifs that lend warmth and handcrafted character. The design has an artisan quality that sets it apart from purely mechanical old-styles.
summary: An artisan old-style serif with genuine calligraphic warmth and an expressive, handcrafted sensibility.
description: Almendra draws on humanist calligraphic traditions to produce a serif that feels simultaneously classical and personal. The moderate contrast and warm stroke movement make it well suited to literary publishing, editorial design, and refined branding where a human touch is valued. Two weights limit its typographic range but each is individually distinctive.

name: Almendra Display
classification_notes: The display companion to Almendra, featuring heightened stroke contrast and more pronounced calligraphic details optimised for large-scale use. Letterforms become more dramatic and decorative at display sizes.
summary: Almendra's display sibling amplifies its calligraphic drama for headline and poster applications.
description: Almendra Display takes the underlying character of the text cut and pushes contrast and detail further, making it unsuitable for small sizes but compelling at headline scale. It is best deployed for chapter headers, poster typography, and decorative display contexts within editorial or literary design. Used in combination with the text weights, it forms a coherent and characterful family.

name: Almendra SC
classification_notes: A small capitals variant of Almendra, providing true-drawn small caps rather than algorithmically scaled versions, maintaining consistent stroke weight and optical size across the letterforms.
summary: A small caps companion to Almendra that provides properly drawn capitals for refined typographic detailing.
description: Almendra SC supplies true small capitals that hold their stroke weight and detail at text sizes, avoiding the crude appearance of algorithmically reduced uppercase letters. It is particularly valuable for editorial typography — running headers, bylines, and drop-cap contexts — within projects already using the Almendra family. Its single weight restricts standalone use but it performs an important supporting role.

name: Alumni Sans
classification_notes: A narrow, high-contrast condensed sans-serif with a wide weight range, drawing on the tradition of collegiate and athletic lettering. Clean vertical stress and tight spacing make it well-suited to compact editorial layouts.
summary: A versatile condensed sans with strong collegiate energy and an impressive weight range including Cyrillic support.
description: Alumni Sans covers nine weights from Thin to Black in a narrow, upright structure that suits headlines, posters, and space-constrained layouts. Its Cyrillic and Cyrillic Extended support broadens its appeal for multilingual publishing. The family works particularly well in editorial contexts where vertical rhythm and typographic density are priorities.

name: Alumni Sans Collegiate One
classification_notes: A bold, single-weight inline collegiate sans with strong horizontal strokes and a distinctly American sports typography heritage. The thick, blocky letterforms are designed for impact at large sizes.
summary: A bold collegiate display face that channels American sports lettering with confident, heavy strokes.
description: Alumni Sans Collegiate One is a single-weight display typeface built around the conventions of varsity and athletic typography. Its heavy, uniform construction gives it strong presence on posters, team branding, and merchandise. Best used at large sizes where its bold geometry can make a clear visual statement.

name: Alumni Sans Inline One
classification_notes: An inline variant of Alumni Sans that uses a contrasting white stroke through the letterforms to create a decorative, high-impact display effect. Intended purely for large-size headline use.
summary: An eye-catching inline display face derived from Alumni Sans, built for bold headlines and decorative titling.
description: Alumni Sans Inline One features a classic inline treatment — a fine white channel running through each stroke — that gives it a distinctive, retro display character. It is available in a single weight and works best at large sizes on posters, covers, and event graphics. Sustained text setting is not its purpose; pure visual impact is.

name: Alumni Sans Pinstripe
classification_notes: An ultra-light, hairline condensed sans with extremely thin strokes that create an elegant pinstripe effect. The single weight is highly specialised, designed for refined, fashion-adjacent display use.
summary: An ultra-thin condensed sans with a delicate pinstripe quality, suited to fashion and luxury display contexts.
description: Alumni Sans Pinstripe reduces the Alumni Sans structure to an extreme hairline weight, creating letterforms of almost architectural fragility. Cyrillic support extends its reach for multilingual fashion and luxury branding. It demands careful use — large sizes, high-quality printing or high-resolution screens — to preserve the refinement of its hairline strokes.

name: Alumni Sans SC
classification_notes: A small caps companion to Alumni Sans, sharing its condensed proportions and wide weight range while replacing lowercase with true small capitals. Designed for typographic refinement in editorial and formal settings.
summary: A small caps variant of Alumni Sans that brings typographic formality and elegance to condensed sans-serif layouts.
description: Alumni Sans SC substitutes true small capitals for lowercase letters across the full nine-weight range, making it a disciplined choice for formal editorial design, certificates, titling, and institutional branding. Its Cyrillic and Cyrillic Extended support mirrors the main family. Used alongside Alumni Sans, it enables nuanced typographic hierarchies with a consistent visual voice.

name: Alyamama
classification_notes: A multi-script serif designed to harmonise Arabic, Greek, and Latin letterforms within a single coherent system. The design balances traditional Arabic calligraphic structure with the proportions of a contemporary Latin serif.
summary: A thoughtfully engineered multi-script serif unifying Arabic, Greek, and Latin in a single harmonious typeface.
description: Alyamama is a rare achievement in multi-script type design, bringing Arabic, Greek, and Latin scripts into visual alignment across seven weights. The Arabic component respects calligraphic traditions while remaining legible at text sizes, and the Latin maintains compatible proportions and contrast. It is well-suited to publishing, academic, and institutional contexts requiring typographic consistency across scripts.

name: Amarante
classification_notes: A decorative serif with strong Art Nouveau influences, featuring organic, floral stroke terminals and elaborate letterform construction. Intended for display use only, with a highly ornamental character.
summary: A richly ornamental Art Nouveau display serif with elaborate terminals that suit vintage and decorative headline work.
description: Amarante draws on the florid aesthetic of turn-of-the-century Art Nouveau typography, with sinuous stroke endings and an overall sense of organic elaboration. It is best reserved for single-word titles, logotypes, and decorative display contexts where its ornamental quality can be appreciated. At body sizes or in extended settings, legibility deteriorates and the decorative intent is lost.

name: Amaranth
classification_notes: A clean, friendly humanist sans-serif with modest contrast and open apertures, available in Regular and Bold. The design sits comfortably between geometric and humanist traditions without strongly emphasising either.
summary: A plain, legible humanist sans-serif that serves reliably as a workmanlike typeface for text and interface use.
description: Amaranth is an unshowy humanist sans with open, readable letterforms that perform consistently at small sizes on screen. Its two-weight family — Regular and Bold — is modest but sufficient for straightforward interface design, web copy, and basic editorial work. It lacks the refinement of premium humanist sans-serifs but offers dependable legibility without stylistic distraction.

name: Amarna
classification_notes: Amarna features clean, minimal strokes with a geometric construction and wide range of weights from Thin to Bold. Its restrained design gives it a contemporary, neutral character suited to modern branding and editorial work.
summary: A clean geometric sans-serif with a broad weight range and a quietly modern personality.
description: Amarna's geometric foundations and minimal detailing make it versatile across both display and text settings. Its extended Latin support broadens its utility for multilingual projects. It works particularly well in branding, editorial layouts, and digital interfaces where a neutral but refined tone is needed.

name: Amatic SC
classification_notes: Amatic SC is a hand-drawn, narrow all-caps typeface with an informal, sketchy quality and uneven stroke widths that evoke casual hand lettering. Its quirky irregularity gives it strong personality at display sizes.
summary: A narrow, hand-lettered all-caps display face with a charming, informal sketchiness.
description: Amatic SC's deliberately rough, uneven strokes give it an authentic hand-drawn warmth that suits casual branding, posters, and decorative headlines. Its Cyrillic and Hebrew script support makes it unusually versatile for a display typeface of this character. It loses legibility quickly at small sizes and is best reserved for short, high-impact text.

name: Amethysta
classification_notes: Amethysta is a single-weight classical serif with high contrast between thick and thin strokes and refined bracketed serifs reminiscent of Renaissance typefaces. Its elegant proportions lean toward display and editorial use rather than extended body text.
summary: A high-contrast classical serif with Renaissance proportions and a refined, bookish elegance.
description: Amethysta's single weight limits its flexibility but makes its design intent clear: this is a typeface built for elegance at larger sizes. Its sharp serifs and strong stroke contrast recall humanist printing types of the fifteenth and sixteenth centuries. It suits editorial headers, fashion contexts, and anywhere a touch of classical distinction is required.

name: Amiko
classification_notes: Amiko is a humanist sans-serif designed with Devanagari script support alongside Latin, giving it a dual-script utility that is relatively rare on Google Fonts. Its open apertures and moderate stroke contrast aid legibility at text sizes.
summary: A humanist sans-serif built for both Devanagari and Latin scripts, prioritising legibility across both.
description: Amiko was designed with Indian-language publishing in mind, making it a practical choice for bilingual or Hindi-language digital content. Its humanist construction keeps it warm and readable at body text sizes in both scripts. The limited weight range of Regular, SemiBold, and Bold covers most typographic hierarchy needs without excess.

name: Amiri
classification_notes: Amiri is a high-quality revival of the Naskh style of Arabic calligraphy with refined Latin accompaniment, offering authentic classical elegance and strong legibility for long-form Arabic text. It was inspired by the type of the early twentieth-century Bulaq Press in Cairo.
summary: A beautifully crafted Naskh revival that brings classical Arabic typographic tradition to digital publishing.
description: Amiri is among the most accomplished Arabic typefaces available on Google Fonts, drawing on the tradition of Bulaq-style Naskh to produce a scholarly yet readable text face. Its accompanying Latin is carefully drawn to harmonise without mimicking the Arabic. It is the obvious choice for Arabic-language books, academic publications, and any project demanding authentic classical Arabic typography.

name: Amiri Quran
classification_notes: Amiri Quran is a specialised variant of Amiri optimised specifically for Quranic text, with precise diacritical marks and spacing conventions faithful to traditional Quran typesetting standards. Its single weight reflects the specific purpose of the design.
summary: A specialised variant of Amiri designed with meticulous attention to the typographic conventions of Quranic text.
description: Amiri Quran goes beyond general Arabic publishing to address the exacting requirements of Quran typesetting, including precise diacritical marks, vowel signs, and traditional spacing conventions. It is a highly specialised tool rather than a general-purpose typeface, and its use outside Quranic or related Islamic scholarly texts would be unusual. For its intended purpose, it is exceptionally well-considered.

name: Amita
classification_notes: Amita is a calligraphic display script supporting both Devanagari and Latin, with flowing strokes that echo traditional Indian pen-written letterforms. Its ornamental quality makes it suited to headlines and decorative applications rather than body text.
summary: A calligraphic display script that bridges Devanagari and Latin traditions with flowing, decorative strokes.
description: Amita's pen-influenced letterforms give it a celebratory, festive quality that translates well to wedding stationery, packaging, and cultural branding projects requiring a South Asian sensibility. The dual-script design means headlines can be set in both Devanagari and Latin without a jarring stylistic disconnect. It is strictly a display face and should not be used for extended text.

name: Anaheim
classification_notes: Anaheim is a sturdy grotesque sans-serif with a generous weight range and Vietnamese script support alongside Latin and Latin Extended. Its straightforward, unpretentious construction gives it workhorse qualities for screen and UI contexts.
summary: A plain, dependable grotesque sans with solid weight coverage and broad multilingual support including Vietnamese.
description: Anaheim offers a no-frills grotesque construction that prioritises function over stylistic distinction, making it a reliable choice for interface design, websites, and informational typography. Its Vietnamese support gives it genuine utility for South-East Asian digital publishing where many grotesques fall short. With five weights from Regular to ExtraBold, it handles most typographic hierarchy demands capably.

name: Ancizar Sans
classification_notes: Ancizar Sans is a humanist sans-serif with optical corrections across its extensive weight range, designed for high legibility in academic and institutional contexts. Its letterforms draw on classical proportions while remaining clean and contemporary.
summary: A rigorous humanist sans with a broad weight range and strong multilingual support across Latin and Greek scripts.
description: Ancizar Sans was developed for institutional use, prioritising legibility and consistency across a wide spectrum of weights from Thin to Black. Its humanist construction gives it warmth without sacrificing the neutrality needed for body text. It suits academic publishing, reports, and multilingual editorial work equally well.

name: Ancizar Serif
classification_notes: Ancizar Serif pairs with its sans counterpart, sharing humanist proportions and optical refinements that make it suitable for long-form reading. Moderate contrast and open apertures contribute to reliable legibility at text sizes.
summary: A companion humanist serif to Ancizar Sans, built for sustained reading with consistent optical balance across seven weights.
description: Ancizar Serif shares the design logic of its sans counterpart, making the two highly effective as a paired type system for publications and academic documents. Its moderate stroke contrast and open letterforms hold up well at small sizes, while the heavier weights provide usable headline options. Greek and extended Latin support makes it a dependable choice for multilingual scholarly publishing.

name: Andada Pro
classification_notes: Andada Pro is a sturdy reading serif with slightly slab-like qualities and robust stroke terminals that give it an earthy, grounded character. Its variable font capabilities and extended language support make it a versatile workhorse for digital editorial work.
summary: A robust reading serif with a warm, grounded character and variable font support, well suited to digital long-form text.
description: Andada Pro evolved from the original Andada, gaining a broader weight range and variable font axes in its Pro iteration. Its sturdy construction and slightly slab-influenced terminals give it excellent durability on screen at body sizes. It works particularly well for digital magazines, news platforms, and any editorial context requiring reliable multilingual text setting.

name: Andika
classification_notes: Andika is specifically designed for literacy and beginner readers, featuring unambiguous single-storey letterforms and generous spacing to reduce confusion between similar characters. It prioritises clarity over stylistic refinement.
summary: A literacy-focused sans-serif designed for beginner readers, with unambiguous letterforms and generous spacing for maximum clarity.
description: Andika was developed by SIL International with the specific goal of supporting early literacy and language learning, making it a specialist tool rather than a general-purpose typeface. Its single-storey 'a' and 'g', open apertures, and wide default spacing minimise the risk of character confusion for developing readers. It is widely used in educational materials, language documentation, and resources for readers with dyslexia.

name: Anek Bangla
classification_notes: Anek Bangla is part of the broader Anek type system, harmonising Bengali script with Latin in a clean, contemporary sans-serif aesthetic. Its wide weight range supports both display and text applications across both scripts.
summary: A contemporary multiscript sans harmonising Bengali and Latin letterforms across a full weight range for versatile digital use.
description: Anek Bangla is one of eight scripts covered by the Anek family, a project aimed at creating a cohesive typographic system for India's diverse linguistic landscape. The Bengali letterforms are designed to share optical weight and spacing rhythm with their Latin counterparts, making mixed-script typesetting visually consistent. It is well suited to apps, websites, and publications serving Bengali-speaking audiences.

name: Anek Devanagari
classification_notes: Anek Devanagari brings the same systematic approach as the wider Anek family to Devanagari script, balancing the script's inherent complexity with clean, neutral sans-serif forms. The weight range from Thin to ExtraBold offers genuine versatility.
summary: A clean, neutral Devanagari sans-serif from the Anek family system, offering a full weight range for both digital and print use.
description: Anek Devanagari tackles one of the most widely used scripts in the world, providing a modern sans-serif option that maintains the structural integrity of Devanagari while integrating harmoniously with Latin. It is part of a coordinated multi-script project making it particularly valuable for pan-Indian digital products and multilingual publishing. The full eight-weight range gives designers meaningful flexibility across headlines, body copy, and UI elements.

name: Anek Gujarati
classification_notes: Anek Gujarati applies the Anek family's unified design approach to Gujarati script, producing clean, optically balanced forms across a wide weight range. The family's systematic construction ensures visual cohesion when Gujarati and Latin are used together.
summary: A systematically designed Gujarati sans-serif from the Anek family, providing cohesive multiscript typography for digital and print.
description: Anek Gujarati extends the Anek project's mission of creating a unified typographic system across India's major scripts, with Gujarati letterforms carefully drawn to match the optical weight and rhythm of the accompanying Latin. The eight-weight range from Thin to ExtraBold makes it adaptable for everything from fine editorial text to bold display use. It is especially useful for businesses and media targeting Gujarati-speaking communities who require professional bilingual typography.

name: Anek Gurmukhi
classification_notes: Anek Gurmukhi covers the Gurmukhi script used for Punjabi within the broader Anek multiscript family, maintaining optical consistency with the Latin component. Clean, neutral forms across a full weight range serve both display and body text requirements.
summary: A clean, professionally crafted Gurmukhi sans-serif within the Anek family, designed for consistent multiscript typesetting.
description: Anek Gurmukhi brings a rigorous, contemporary sans-serif sensibility to the Gurmukhi script used primarily for the Punjabi language, integrating seamlessly with its Latin companion through shared optical weight and spacing logic. As part of the coordinated Anek system, it is a natural fit for pan-Indian digital platforms, government communications, and multilingual publications. The full eight-weight range ensures usability across a wide variety of typographic contexts.

name: Anek Kannada
classification_notes: Anek Kannada is part of the broader Anek multi-script superfamily, designed to harmonise Kannada script forms with Latin counterparts. The design features open apertures and a contemporary humanist sensibility suited to screen use.
summary: A versatile multi-weight sans-serif that brings Kannada and Latin scripts into a unified, contemporary design system.
description: Part of the extensive Anek superfamily from Ek Type, this face spans eight weights and handles both Kannada and Latin with consistent optical balance. Its humanist character makes it suitable for digital interfaces, editorial layouts, and any context requiring bilingual Kannada–Latin typesetting. The broad weight range gives designers genuine flexibility from delicate display sizes down to functional body text.

name: Anek Latin
classification_notes: Anek Latin is the Latin-primary member of the Anek superfamily, featuring slightly low-contrast strokes, open counters, and friendly proportions. It extends to Vietnamese, reflecting the project's commitment to broad script coverage.
summary: The Latin flagship of the Anek superfamily, offering a clean humanist sans with strong multilingual reach across eight weights.
description: Anek Latin covers Latin and Latin Extended alongside Vietnamese, making it one of the more linguistically inclusive entries in the family. Its moderate x-height, open apertures, and restrained personality make it a solid workhorse for interfaces, apps, and editorial projects that need neutral yet warm typography. The eight-weight range, from Thin to ExtraBold, ensures it can handle headline and body roles within a single cohesive system.

name: Anek Malayalam
classification_notes: Anek Malayalam pairs Malayalam script with Latin in a unified sans-serif system, respecting the distinctive curved loops and horizontal flow of Malayalam letterforms. The design maintains consistent weight contrast across both scripts.
summary: A carefully unified sans-serif pairing Malayalam and Latin scripts across a full eight-weight range.
description: Malayalam's characteristically curved, loop-rich letterforms present a real challenge for cross-script harmonisation, and Anek Malayalam handles this with considered stroke weights and consistent optical sizing. The result is a dependable face for digital products, publications, and signage serving Malayalam-speaking audiences alongside Latin readers. Like all Anek members, its breadth of weights makes it adaptable from headlines to captions.

name: Anek Odia
classification_notes: Anek Odia integrates the rounded, complex letterforms of the Odia (Oriya) script with matching Latin within the Anek design system. Stroke terminals and weight distribution are calibrated to respect Odia's distinctive circular structures.
summary: An eight-weight sans-serif uniting the rounded complexity of Odia script with a harmonious Latin companion.
description: Odia is notable for its circular letterforms and overhead arc strokes, and Anek Odia preserves these characteristics while aligning them optically with its Latin counterpart. This makes it well suited to bilingual publishing, government communications, and digital platforms serving Odisha's population. The full weight range from Thin to ExtraBold provides ample scope for typographic hierarchy in both scripts.

name: Anek Tamil
classification_notes: Anek Tamil brings Tamil's angular yet flowing script into the Anek multi-script framework, balancing the script's characteristic horizontal stroke flow with a clean, contemporary sans construction. Latin weights are optically matched to Tamil across all eight variants.
summary: A well-resolved Tamil and Latin sans-serif pairing drawn from a single coherent design system across eight weights.
description: Tamil's distinct combination of curves and angular terminals requires careful translation into a sans-serif framework, and Anek Tamil manages this with consistent stroke contrast and clear counters. It performs well in digital and print contexts where Tamil and Latin must coexist without one script visually dominating the other. The eight-weight span makes it suitable for everything from mobile UI text to large-format headlines.

name: Anek Telugu
classification_notes: Anek Telugu harmonises the looping, voluminous letterforms of Telugu script with its Latin companion under the unified Anek aesthetic. Stroke weight and spacing are calibrated to ensure legibility in both scripts at small and large sizes.
summary: A thoughtfully unified Telugu and Latin sans-serif spanning eight weights within the cohesive Anek superfamily.
description: Telugu's large, rounded letterforms and numerous conjunct characters make multi-script harmonisation particularly demanding, and Anek Telugu addresses this through careful counter sizing and consistent weight contrast. It is well positioned for digital platforms, newspapers, and institutional communications serving Telugu-speaking audiences. As with all Anek members, the full weight range delivers genuine typographic versatility within a single family.

name: Angkor
classification_notes: Angkor is a decorative display face for the Khmer script with a single Regular weight, featuring traditionally styled letterforms with pronounced ornamentation suited to titles and ceremonial contexts. Latin support is basic and secondary.
summary: A decorative Khmer display face with a strong traditional character, best used at large sizes for headings and ceremonial contexts.
description: Angkor draws on classical Khmer letterform traditions, giving it a distinctly ornate quality that sets it apart from workhorse Khmer text faces. Its single weight limits its versatility considerably, positioning it squarely as a headline or display choice rather than a text companion. It suits cultural projects, signage, and Cambodian-language editorial contexts where a sense of heritage and formality is desired.

name: Annapurna SIL
classification_notes: Annapurna SIL is a professionally crafted Devanagari serif designed by SIL International for high legibility in extended text, with a scholarly Latin companion sharing similar proportions and stroke character. It prioritises readability for linguistic and Bible translation work.
summary: A rigorously legible Devanagari and Latin serif from SIL International, purpose-built for extended text in academic and linguistic contexts.
description: Annapurna SIL was developed to meet the demanding requirements of Bible translation and linguistic publication, where consistency and readability across long texts are paramount. The Devanagari design is carefully drawn with well-proportioned vowel marks and conjuncts, while the Latin companion complements it without overwhelming. It is an authoritative choice for scholarly publishing, dictionaries, and any project requiring dependable Devanagari text setting.

name: Annie Use Your Telescope
classification_notes: A casual, hand-lettered script that mimics the informal writing of a schoolchild or amateur penman. Irregular baseline and loose letterforms give it a spontaneous, personal quality.
summary: A charmingly wonky handwritten script that captures the feel of a personal, unpractised hand.
description: Annie Use Your Telescope mimics informal handwriting with deliberately uneven strokes and a loose, unpolished rhythm. It suits children's projects, personal branding, greeting cards, and any context requiring a warm, approachable tone. Its single weight limits versatility, making it best deployed at display sizes for short passages of text.

name: Anonymous Pro
classification_notes: A fixed-width typeface designed specifically for coding and terminal use, featuring clear disambiguation between easily confused characters such as 0/O and l/1/I. It includes both roman and italic styles with strong legibility at small sizes.
summary: A dependable monospace typeface built for coding and terminal work, with excellent character disambiguation and multilingual support.
description: Anonymous Pro was designed by Mark Simonson with programmers firmly in mind, offering four styles — regular, italic, bold, and bold italic — across Cyrillic, Greek, and Latin scripts. The design pays careful attention to characters that are frequently confused in code environments, making it a reliable workhorse for developers. Its straightforward, unshowy aesthetic also makes it effective for technical documentation and interface contexts.

name: Anta
classification_notes: A contemporary geometric sans with slightly technical, angular details that give it a distinctive edge beyond typical neutral grotesques. The design balances modernity with functional legibility across digital contexts.
summary: A crisp geometric sans with subtle technical character, suited to modern digital branding and display use.
description: Anta offers a clean geometric foundation with restrained angular details that lend it a slightly futuristic or technical personality. Its single weight limits its utility in complex typographic hierarchies, but it performs well at headline sizes in digital contexts. The inclusion of mathematical character support broadens its appeal for technical and scientific publishing.

name: Antic
classification_notes: A simple, unpretentious humanist sans-serif with minimal stylistic flourishes and an even, open texture. It reads cleanly at text sizes despite its single-weight limitation.
summary: A plain, functional humanist sans that prioritises legibility over personality.
description: Antic is an unassuming sans-serif that draws on humanist traditions to deliver clean, readable text without drawing attention to itself. The single Regular weight restricts its use in editorial contexts requiring typographic hierarchy. It is best suited to lightweight web text or situations where simplicity and economy are paramount.

name: Antic Didone
classification_notes: A high-contrast modern serif in the Didone tradition, featuring strong vertical stress, hairline serifs, and pronounced thick-to-thin stroke contrast. The single weight limits its versatility but the design carries genuine elegance.
summary: A refined Didone serif with dramatic stroke contrast, evoking the elegance of classical modern type.
description: Antic Didone follows the tradition of Bodoni and Didot, presenting sharp hairline serifs and vertical stress with a high degree of thick-to-thin contrast. It is well suited to fashion editorial, luxury branding, and headline use where typographic drama is desirable. The absence of additional weights means it functions best as a display face rather than a workhorse for extended text.

name: Antic Slab
classification_notes: A sturdy slab serif with even stroke weights and squared serifs, offering a solid, grounded texture on the page. The design leans toward the geometric end of the slab spectrum without being overly rigid.
summary: A solid, dependable slab serif that handles body text with unpretentious reliability.
description: Antic Slab provides the sturdy structure of an Egyptian slab serif in a single weight suited to text-size reading. Its consistent stroke weight and squared terminals give it a grounded, no-nonsense quality that works well in editorial and print contexts. The limitation of a single weight means it is best paired with contrasting display faces to establish clear typographic hierarchy.

name: Anton
classification_notes: An extremely condensed, heavy sans-serif with tight letterforms and minimal spacing, designed purely for maximum impact at large display sizes. Its narrow proportions and bold weight make it one of the most immediately recognisable typefaces on Google Fonts.
summary: An unapologetically bold, condensed display sans built for high-impact headlines and posters.
description: Anton is one of Google Fonts' most widely used display typefaces, and for good reason — its extreme condensation and heavy weight pack enormous visual punch into a compact horizontal space. Designed by Vernon Adams, it is ideal for posters, social media graphics, sports branding, and any headline context demanding immediate attention. The single weight is the only option, but it is so focused in purpose that the limitation rarely matters.

name: Anton SC
classification_notes: A small-caps companion to Anton, sharing its extreme condensation and heavy weight but rendered entirely in capital forms scaled to x-height. It carries the same blunt, high-impact character with an added formality from the all-caps construction.
summary: Anton SC delivers the same punchy condensed weight as Anton but in an all-small-caps format for formal display use.
description: Anton SC is a small-caps variant of the popular Anton display typeface, offering the same tightly condensed, heavy proportions in a capitalised form that suits formal or institutional headline contexts. It is suited to the same use cases as Anton — posters, sports graphics, editorial splashes — while giving a slightly more composed typographic feel through its all-caps uniformity. Like Anton, it is available in a single weight, confirming its identity as a dedicated display tool rather than a versatile text family.

name: Antonio
classification_notes: Antonio is a narrow, high-contrast display sans-serif with strong vertical stress and sharp, clean terminals. Its condensed proportions and wide weight range make it well-suited to headlines and sporting or editorial contexts.
summary: A sharp, condensed sans-serif built for commanding headlines with a distinctly athletic, editorial edge.
description: Antonio's tight proportions and high contrast give it a punchy presence at large sizes, making it a natural fit for sports branding, magazine headlines, and poster work. Its range from Thin to Bold offers considerable flexibility without sacrificing its core character. It performs best when given space to breathe at display sizes rather than set tight in body copy.

name: Anuphan
classification_notes: Anuphan is a clean, modern humanist sans-serif designed with careful attention to both Latin and Thai script harmonisation. Its even stroke weight and open apertures prioritise legibility across both writing systems.
summary: A well-mannered humanist sans designed to serve Thai and Latin scripts with equal care and consistency.
description: Anuphan addresses the often-awkward typographic challenge of mixing Thai and Latin text by designing both scripts to share a common visual rhythm and weight. Its neutral, open character makes it a solid choice for user interfaces, web applications, and editorial text in bilingual contexts. The full seven-weight range gives designers practical flexibility for establishing clear typographic hierarchy.

name: Anybody
classification_notes: Anybody is a highly expressive variable display typeface with an unusually wide axis spanning ultra-condensed to ultra-extended proportions. Its geometric construction and bold personality make it a strong choice for experimental headline work.
summary: A versatile variable display sans that stretches from ultra-condensed to ultra-extended, inviting bold typographic experimentation.
description: Anybody's primary appeal lies in its variable width axis, which gives designers an unusually broad range of expressive possibilities within a single typeface. Its clean geometric forms hold up well at both extremes, making it suited to poster design, branding, and editorial display where dramatic proportion shifts are part of the concept. It is firmly a display face and is not intended for extended reading.

name: Aoboshi One
classification_notes: Aoboshi One is a single-weight Japanese Mincho-style serif designed for display use, with elegant thin-to-thick stroke contrast characteristic of the genre. Its Latin companion is designed to harmonise with the Japanese glyphs in mixed-script settings.
summary: An elegant single-weight Mincho serif crafted for Japanese display typography with a refined Latin companion.
description: Aoboshi One draws on the traditional Mincho style, with the characteristic high contrast and fine bracketed serifs that give Japanese editorial typography its distinctive formality. Its single weight limits flexibility but concentrates its strengths at display sizes, where its delicate stroke modulation reads with clarity and grace. It suits editorial headlines, book covers, and any project calling for a refined Japanese typographic voice.

name: Arapey
classification_notes: Arapey is an elegant, single-weight transitional serif with moderate contrast and a slightly narrow set, lending it a refined editorial quality. Its design draws on classical book typography traditions, prioritising readability and quiet sophistication.
summary: A refined single-weight transitional serif with quiet elegance suited to editorial and fashion contexts.
description: Arapey sits comfortably within the tradition of classical book serifs, offering moderate stroke contrast and clean bracketed serifs that work well in long-form editorial text. Its slightly narrow proportions give it a subtle chic quality that makes it a reasonable choice for fashion editorial or literary publishing. The absence of a bold weight limits its versatility for projects requiring strong typographic hierarchy.

name: Arbutus
classification_notes: Arbutus is an unconventional display serif with a hand-crafted, slightly rough quality and distinctive decorative details reminiscent of wood-type and letterpress printing. Its irregular texture gives it a warm, artisanal character quite distinct from polished digital typefaces.
summary: A characterful display serif with a deliberate hand-crafted roughness that evokes letterpress and wood-type printing traditions.
description: Arbutus brings a warm, artisanal texture to display typography, with slightly irregular strokes and distinctive serifs that suggest a physical printing history. It suits branding for craft businesses, vintage-inspired poster work, and editorial contexts where a sense of handmade authenticity is desirable. Its single weight and display-focused design mean it is best deployed sparingly at large sizes rather than used for body text.

name: Arbutus Slab
classification_notes: Arbutus Slab shares the warm, slightly rough texture of its companion Arbutus but applies it to a sturdy slab serif construction with low contrast and robust letterforms. The result is an approachable, characterful slab with genuine personality.
summary: A sturdy slab serif with the same hand-crafted warmth as Arbutus, offering a more readable companion for text-heavy display settings.
description: Arbutus Slab translates the artisanal quality of Arbutus into a more workable slab format, with its low-contrast strokes and square serifs providing better colour consistency across a line of text.
