description: Fasthand's informal, hand-lettered feel makes it suitable for casual branding, packaging, and social media graphics where approachability is valued. Its Khmer support is genuinely notable, broadening its utility for projects targeting Southeast Asian audiences. The single weight limits its range, but its dual-script capability gives it a practical edge that purely decorative faces lack.

name: Fauna One
classification_notes: Fauna One is a refined, low-contrast humanist serif with open apertures and elegant proportions that make it highly readable at text sizes. Its restrained character gives it a quiet sophistication suited to sustained reading.
summary: A quietly elegant humanist serif designed for comfortable reading, with open forms and balanced proportions.
description: Fauna One occupies a useful middle ground between classical book serifs and contemporary screen-optimised typefaces, making it a solid choice for editorial body text, longform digital publishing, and refined branding applications. Its single regular weight is a practical limitation that rules out typographic hierarchies within the family alone, but paired with a complementary sans it performs well. The generous x-height and open counters support readability across screen sizes.

name: Faustina
classification_notes: Faustina is a workhorse text serif with moderate contrast and sturdy letterforms designed specifically for screen legibility across a wide range of sizes and weights. Its extended weight range and Vietnamese support mark it out as a genuinely practical family.
summary: A robust, screen-optimised text serif with a broad weight range and strong multilingual support, built for serious publishing work.
description: Faustina was developed with legibility under demanding digital conditions as its primary brief, resulting in sturdy strokes, clear differentiation between characters, and a weight range that spans from Light to ExtraBold for genuine typographic flexibility. Vietnamese support alongside Latin and Latin Extended makes it more versatile than many of its peers for multilingual editorial projects. It suits online news, long-form articles, and any context where reliable, unfussy text performance is the priority.

name: Federant
classification_notes: Federant is an ornate, historically influenced display serif with uncial and blackletter-adjacent qualities, evoking medieval manuscript lettering. Its decorative complexity makes it highly specialised and difficult to use outside narrow historical or themed contexts.
summary: An ornate, manuscript-inspired display face that reaches back to uncial and early blackletter traditions for a highly decorative effect.
description: Federant's elaborate, historically charged letterforms make it a one-trick pony, but within its specific niche — period-themed book covers, fantasy branding, heraldic design, or historical event graphics — it delivers genuine atmosphere. The single weight and extreme decorative nature mean it must be used at large sizes and in very small doses. It is not a typeface for the faint-hearted or the generalist designer.

name: Federo
classification_notes: Federo is a clean, understated humanist sans-serif with subtle calligraphic underpinnings that give its letterforms quiet personality without tipping into expressiveness. It sits comfortably in the space between geometric neutrality and organic warmth.
summary: A calm, humanist sans-serif with gentle calligraphic traces that make it readable and quietly distinctive without drawing attention to itself.
description: Federo's restrained design makes it a dependable choice for editorial text, UI copy, and branding contexts where legibility and a touch of warmth are needed without expressive flourish. Its single weight is a real practical constraint, limiting its usefulness in complex typographic systems that demand hierarchy through weight variation. Nonetheless, as a text face in light-weight contexts or combined with a contrasting display typeface, it performs solidly.

name: Felipa
classification_notes: Felipa is a loose, informal brush script with a hand-drawn quality and irregular baseline. The letterforms have expressive, somewhat scratchy strokes that convey spontaneity.
summary: A loose, hand-drawn brush script with an energetic, informal character.
description: Felipa's irregular strokes and casual letterforms give it an authentic handmade feel, suitable for short display applications where personality is paramount. It works best at large sizes for greeting cards, invitations, or lifestyle branding. Its single weight limits versatility, making it a one-role typeface.

name: Fenix
classification_notes: Fenix features moderate contrast between thick and thin strokes with sturdy serifs, sitting between old-style and modern classifications. Its robust construction makes it legible at smaller sizes despite being a single-weight design.
summary: A solid single-weight transitional serif with balanced proportions and reliable legibility.
description: Fenix occupies a dependable middle ground in the serif spectrum, drawing on classical proportions without leaning into either old-style warmth or modern severity. It suits editorial body text, long-form reading, and print-oriented design work. The lack of additional weights constrains its typographic flexibility in complex hierarchies.

name: Festive
classification_notes: Festive is a flowing, decorative calligraphic script with pronounced swashes and ornamental letterforms. It leans heavily into celebration and occasion-driven aesthetics.
summary: An ornamental calligraphic script designed for celebratory and decorative headline use.
description: Festive's sweeping swashes and flourished capitals make it well-suited to seasonal graphics, invitations, and occasion branding where exuberance is the goal. Its Vietnamese script support is a notable practical advantage over similar display scripts. As a single-weight design it is firmly a display-only typeface.

name: Figtree
classification_notes: Figtree is a clean geometric sans-serif with softened terminals and friendly, open apertures that temper its geometric underpinning. Its wide weight range and optical balance make it versatile across UI and editorial contexts.
summary: A polished geometric sans with soft edges and a broad weight range suited to modern digital interfaces.
description: Figtree blends geometric structure with humanist warmth through its subtly rounded terminals, resulting in a typeface that feels approachable without being childish. Its seven weights give designers strong typographic range for hierarchy in apps, websites, and branding. It competes comfortably with popular choices like Inter and DM Sans in the UI sans-serif category.

name: Finger Paint
classification_notes: Finger Paint mimics the unsteady, globular letterforms of finger-painted text, with thick irregular strokes and childlike proportions. It is a highly specific novelty typeface with a very narrow range of appropriate applications.
summary: A chunky novelty display face imitating the look of finger-painted lettering.
description: Finger Paint's thick, wobbly strokes and naive letterforms are immediately evocative of children's art, making it appropriate for educational materials, children's brands, and playful event graphics. Its strong personality severely limits context — it reads as tone-deaf in anything requiring professionalism. A single weight and Latin-only support reinforce its very narrow use case.

name: Finlandica Headline
classification_notes: Finlandica Headline is designed for large-scale display use, with optically adjusted spacing and slightly more pronounced contrast than its text counterpart. It supports Cyrillic extended, making it genuinely useful across a broad linguistic range.
summary: A display-optimised humanist sans with strong Cyrillic support, built for headlines and large-scale type.
description: Finlandica Headline is the large-size counterpart to Finlandica Text, fine-tuned for headline weights and optical performance at display sizes. Its broad weight range from Thin to Black offers serious typographic power for editorial and branding applications. Cyrillic and Cyrillic Extended support makes it a practical choice for multilingual publishing.

name: Finlandica Text
classification_notes: Finlandica Text is optimised for body copy and extended reading, with generous x-height, open apertures, and spacing tuned for smaller sizes. Its Cyrillic support distinguishes it from many text sans-serifs in this segment.
summary: A reliable text-optimised humanist sans with comprehensive Cyrillic support for serious reading environments.
description: Finlandica Text is designed with legibility at small sizes as its primary concern, featuring open counters and neutral letterforms that sustain reading over long passages. Paired with its headline sibling, it provides a cohesive typographic system for multilingual editorial and digital projects. The nine-weight range makes it one of the more fully featured text sans options on Google Fonts.

name: Fira Code
classification_notes: Fira Code extends the Fira Mono typeface with programming ligatures that render common multi-character coding symbols as unified glyphs. It has a clean, neutral construction well-suited to extended on-screen reading in code editors.
summary: The definitive programming font for ligature enthusiasts, combining clean monospaced construction with contextual coding symbols.
description: Fira Code is based on Fira Mono and adds an extensive set of programming ligatures that transform operator sequences like `=>` and `!=` into single cohesive glyphs, reducing visual noise in code. Its neutral letterforms and strong legibility at small screen sizes make it one of the most popular choices for development environments worldwide. Cyrillic and Greek support extend its usefulness beyond purely Latin-script codebases.

name: Fira Mono
classification_notes: Fira Mono is a monospaced companion to Fira Sans, retaining humanist proportions and open apertures that improve legibility in code editors. Its three-weight range offers flexibility without sacrificing the warmth characteristic of the Fira family.
summary: A humanist monospaced typeface designed for code, offering warmth and legibility that most mono fonts lack.
description: Commissioned by Mozilla for the Firefox OS project, Fira Mono brings the humanist sensibility of Fira Sans into a fixed-width format. Its open counters and clear letterforms make it particularly well suited to coding environments and terminal interfaces. Broad script support including Cyrillic and Greek extends its usefulness across international developer tooling.

name: Fira Sans
classification_notes: Fira Sans draws on humanist sans-serif traditions with open apertures, slightly calligraphic details, and strong differentiation between characters, making it highly legible at small sizes. Its exceptionally large weight range and script coverage reflect its origins as a system UI typeface.
summary: A comprehensive humanist sans-serif built for Firefox OS that excels as a workhorse UI and text typeface across multiple scripts.
description: Commissioned by Mozilla, Fira Sans was designed to be both functional and characterful, balancing screen readability with a warm humanist personality. Its nine weights, paired with extensive Cyrillic, Greek, and Latin Extended coverage, make it one of the more versatile open-source sans-serifs available. It performs reliably across interface design, editorial body text, and branding applications where neutrality with a hint of character is required.

name: Fira Sans Condensed
classification_notes: Fira Sans Condensed maintains the humanist character and open apertures of the standard Fira Sans while reducing horizontal width for space-constrained settings. The full nine-weight range is preserved, giving designers considerable typographic range in compressed layouts.
summary: The condensed cut of Fira Sans retains all its humanist warmth while delivering a narrower footprint suitable for headlines and dense layouts.
description: Fira Sans Condensed is a direct companion to the standard Fira Sans, sharing its open letterforms and broad script support in a tighter width. It is well suited to headline and subheading use where column widths are restricted, as well as navigation labels and UI components under space pressure. The complete weight range from Thin to Black gives it genuine flexibility for typographic hierarchy in condensed settings.

name: Fira Sans Extra Condensed
classification_notes: Fira Sans Extra Condensed pushes the family's width reduction further than the Condensed cut, making it suitable for very tight settings such as data tables, narrow columns, and poster headlines. Humanist letter details are retained despite the extreme compression.
summary: The most compressed member of the Fira Sans family, designed for extreme space efficiency without losing its humanist character.
description: Fira Sans Extra Condensed serves the narrow end of the Fira family's width spectrum, ideal for poster headlines, narrow editorial columns, and data-dense interfaces. Its humanist proportions and open apertures help preserve legibility even at very tight settings where many condensed typefaces break down. The full nine-weight range and multilingual script support make it a practical choice for international projects requiring maximum typographic flexibility in minimal space.

name: Fjalla One
classification_notes: Fjalla One is a single-weight condensed sans-serif with strong vertical stress, high contrast between strokes, and tight proportions that give it an imposing presence at display sizes. Its design sits between grotesque and gothic influences, lending it a bold editorial character.
summary: A high-impact single-weight condensed sans-serif built for bold headlines and display use.
description: Fjalla One's tight proportions and high contrast make it a reliable choice for newspaper headlines, poster typography, and editorial splash pages where strong visual presence is required. As a single regular weight it offers no flexibility in weight hierarchy, so it pairs best with a complementary text face. Its extended Latin and Cyrillic coverage broadens its practical application across European and Slavic language contexts.

name: Fjord One
classification_notes: Fjord One is a single-weight serif with moderate contrast, slightly bracketed serifs, and refined proportions that place it in the transitional tradition. Its restrained design gives it a literary quality suited to long-form reading.
summary: A refined single-weight transitional serif with a quiet, literary quality suited to body text and editorial settings.
description: Fjord One draws on classical transitional serif conventions, offering moderate stroke contrast and carefully proportioned letterforms that support comfortable extended reading. Its single weight limits its typographic range, making it best deployed as a body or text face paired with a contrasting display or sans-serif companion. The restrained design works well in book layouts, long-form editorial pieces, and any context where legibility and understated elegance are priorities.

name: Flamenco
classification_notes: Flamenco is an ornamental display typeface with pointed, angular terminals and a strong Art Deco influence, suggesting hand-lettered Spanish or Latin signage. Its decorative flourishes make it highly expressive but narrow in practical application.
summary: A pointed, Art Deco-inflected display typeface with strong decorative character evoking vintage Spanish signage and poster lettering.
description: Flamenco's angular terminals and ornate detailing give it an unmistakable personality rooted in early twentieth-century decorative lettering traditions. It is most effective at large display sizes — posters, event graphics, album covers, and branding projects that call for a vintage or Iberian flavour. Its limited two-weight range and narrow Latin-only script coverage restrict it to specialised decorative use rather than any functional typographic role.

name: Flavors
classification_notes: Flavors is a highly decorative display face with outlined, multiline strokes that create a layered, retro candy-stripe effect. Its single weight and overtly novelty character place it firmly in the one-trick display category.
summary: A novelty display typeface with layered stripe detailing that channels retro fairground and confectionery lettering traditions.
description: Flavors uses multiple parallel outlines within each letterform to create a bold, candy-striped visual effect that reads as immediately retro and playful. It is best suited to short headline bursts on posters, event flyers, packaging, and social media graphics where maximum decorative impact is the goal. Its appeal is narrow and contextual — outside of deliberately kitsch or nostalgic design projects, it has very limited application.

name: Fleur De Leah
classification_notes: A flowing, cursive script with elegant, loose letterforms reminiscent of casual handwriting. The strokes have a natural, relaxed quality with moderate contrast.
summary: A graceful, free-flowing cursive script with an effortlessly casual charm.
description: Fleur De Leah mimics natural handwriting with loose, connected letterforms that feel personal and warm. It suits wedding stationery, lifestyle branding, and social media graphics where an approachable, handcrafted aesthetic is desired. Its Vietnamese and Latin Extended support broadens its appeal across multilingual projects.

name: Flow Block
classification_notes: An experimental display typeface built from solid rectangular block elements, creating a pixelated, modular aesthetic. Letterforms are constructed entirely from uniform square units with no curves.
summary: A fully modular block typeface where every letterform is assembled from uniform square units.
description: Flow Block reduces the alphabet to its most elemental geometric form, constructing letters from a strict grid of solid rectangles. The effect is bold, digital, and distinctly retro, evoking early pixel art and LED signage. It works best at large sizes for posters, experimental branding, or motion graphics where its rigid structure reads as a deliberate design statement.

name: Flow Circular
classification_notes: A companion to Flow Block that constructs letterforms from circular dot or arc units, producing a soft, bubbly, modular aesthetic. The circular modules replace the hard edges of its sibling with rounded forms.
summary: A dot-matrix inspired display face built entirely from circular modules for a playful, retro feel.
description: Flow Circular assembles each letter from uniform circular elements, creating a halftone or LED-dot aesthetic that sits somewhere between digital nostalgia and contemporary graphic design. It carries an inherently playful, friendly tone that distinguishes it from the harder-edged Flow Block. Best deployed at large display sizes in posters, titles, or experimental visual contexts.

name: Flow Rounded
classification_notes: The third variant in the Flow series, this face uses rounded rectangular or pill-shaped modules to construct letterforms, blending the solidity of Flow Block with the softness of Flow Circular.
summary: A rounded modular display typeface that softens the rigid block construction of the Flow series into approachable pill-shaped units.
description: Flow Rounded occupies the middle ground in the Flow family, using rounded rectangular segments to build each letterform with a friendlier, less severe character than Flow Block. The consistent module size gives it a disciplined, grid-based logic while the rounded corners add warmth. It suits playful branding, children's media, or any headline context calling for structured yet inviting display lettering.

name: Foldit
classification_notes: An unusual display typeface with letterforms that appear folded or creased, as though the type has been constructed from bent paper or ribbon. The optical illusion of dimensionality is central to its design.
summary: A distinctive display face whose letterforms simulate the appearance of folded or interlocking ribbon, creating a three-dimensional optical effect.
description: Foldit is built around a single visual conceit — letters that look as though they have been folded back on themselves, producing an interlocking, bas-relief quality. Its broad weight range from Thin to Black gives designers genuine flexibility across different contexts, from delicate decorative headings to bold, impactful poster work. It is best used sparingly where the folded illusion can be appreciated, such as logos, packaging, or editorial headlines.

name: Fondamento
classification_notes: A formal, pen-drawn calligraphic script with pronounced thick-thin stroke contrast and sharp terminals that reference traditional Italian chancery or humanist calligraphy. Letterforms are upright with elegant, structured strokes.
summary: A refined calligraphic script rooted in the humanist chancery tradition, with crisp stroke contrast and formal elegance.
description: Fondamento draws directly from pen-and-ink calligraphic traditions, with letterforms that balance formal discipline and handcrafted warmth. Its pronounced thick-thin contrast and sharp pen terminals give it a distinctly artisanal, craft quality suited to book covers, wine labels, certificates, and heritage branding. It reads well at display sizes but retains enough clarity for short passages of decorative text.

name: Fontdiner Swanky
classification_notes: A bold, exuberant display face designed to evoke mid-century American diner and doo-wop signage, with chunky letterforms, quirky details, and a self-consciously retro personality.
summary: An unabashedly retro display typeface channelling the exuberant spirit of 1950s American diner and pop culture graphics.
description: Fontdiner Swanky leans fully into mid-century American kitsch, with letterforms that reference neon signage, jukebox typography, and drive-in culture. Its single-weight design is unapologetically decorative, making it unsuitable for body text but highly effective for themed branding, event posters, menus, and retro packaging. It is a one-trick typeface, but it executes that trick with committed enthusiasm.

name: Forum
classification_notes: A single-weight display face drawing from classical Roman inscription lettering and early Renaissance type, with elegant proportions, moderate stroke contrast, and open, spacious letterforms. Cyrillic support is included.
summary: An elegant single-weight serif rooted in classical Roman proportions, offering a timeless, epigraphic quality ideal for cultural and editorial contexts.
description: Forum takes its cues from inscriptional Roman lettering and early humanist typefaces, resulting in open, well-proportioned letterforms with restrained stroke contrast and refined details. Its single weight limits versatility but reinforces a sense of considered, understated authority well-suited to book covers, museum materials, cultural institutions, and editorial headlines. Cyrillic and extended Latin support make it genuinely useful across European multilingual projects.

name: Fragment Mono
classification_notes: Fragment Mono combines monospace structure with subtle serif detailing, giving it a refined, technical character. Its even spacing and clear letterforms make it well-suited to code display and editorial contexts.
summary: A monospace typeface with understated serif detailing that bridges the gap between code display and editorial use.
description: Fragment Mono's structured spacing and clean construction make it a reliable choice for code editors and technical documentation. The subtle serif touches lend it a distinctly literary quality uncommon in monospace designs. Its Cyrillic Extended support broadens its appeal for multilingual technical projects.

name: Francois One
classification_notes: Francois One is a bold, condensed grotesque with strong optical weight and tight spacing, designed primarily for headlines and display use. Its single weight forces a confident, impactful presence on the page.
summary: A single-weight display grotesque built for maximum headline impact with minimal fuss.
description: Francois One's heavy proportions and tight letterspacing make it immediately arresting at large sizes, ideal for posters, banners, and editorial headlines. Its lack of weight variants encourages designers to use it decisively rather than sparingly. The clean grotesque structure keeps it versatile across branding and web headline contexts.

name: Frank Ruhl Libre
classification_notes: Frank Ruhl Libre is a classical Hebrew and Latin serif with warm, humanist proportions rooted in traditional Hebrew book typography. Its generous weight range makes it highly adaptable across both text and display settings.
summary: A classically grounded serif typeface designed with equal care for Hebrew and Latin scripts, rooted in the tradition of fine book typography.
description: Frank Ruhl Libre draws from the legacy of Frank Rühl, a foundational Hebrew typeface from the early twentieth century, while modernising it for contemporary digital use. Its warm proportions and strong vertical rhythm make it an excellent choice for long-form reading in Hebrew and Latin texts alike. The wide weight range — from Light to Black — extends its usefulness from body copy through to bold display headings.

name: Fraunces
classification_notes: Fraunces is an optically variable 'wonky' serif with deliberate quirks in letterform construction, drawing on late nineteenth-century display type traditions. Its optical size axis and softly irregular curves give it a distinctive, expressive warmth.
summary: A characterful variable serif with deliberate optical quirks that make it one of the most distinctive display typefaces on Google Fonts.
description: Fraunces was designed with a so-called 'wonk' axis, allowing letters to shift between orthodox and idiosyncratic forms, giving designers precise control over personality. Its high contrast and soft, slightly swollen curves reference late Victorian display types while feeling entirely contemporary. It suits editorial mastheads, branding projects, and any context demanding a serif with genuine visual character.

name: Freckle Face
classification_notes: Freckle Face is a chunky, irregular display typeface with a hand-drawn, childlike quality, mimicking the uneven strokes of a broad marker or crayon. Its single weight and novelty construction limit it strictly to decorative and playful contexts.
summary: A boisterous, hand-drawn display face with deliberately rough edges designed for playful, child-friendly contexts.
description: Freckle Face leans fully into its hand-made aesthetic, with irregular stroke widths and uneven baselines that evoke chalk or crayon lettering. It is best suited to children's publishing, event posters, and packaging where an exuberant, informal tone is needed. Its single weight and limited versatility mean it functions as a one-use decorative tool rather than a workhorse.

name: Fredericka the Great
classification_notes: Fredericka the Great is an ornate display typeface featuring inline and engraved detailing within heavy letterforms, reminiscent of Victorian wood type and letterpress printing. Its decorative interior texture makes it highly distinctive but narrowly applicable.
summary: A Victorian-inspired display face with engraved inline detailing that channels the drama of nineteenth-century wood type.
description: Fredericka the Great pairs bold, heavy outer strokes with intricate interior line patterns that recall letterpress and engraving traditions. It performs best at large display sizes where the internal texture is clearly visible, making it ideal for poster headlines, signage, and decorative branding. Its single weight and highly specific aesthetic confine it to contexts where nostalgia and ornament are the primary goal.

name: Fredoka
classification_notes: Fredoka is a friendly rounded sans-serif with soft terminals and open apertures that give it an approachable, contemporary personality. Its support for both Hebrew and Latin scripts, combined with a useful weight range, makes it a practical choice for inclusive digital interfaces.
summary: A warm rounded sans-serif with broad script support and a friendly disposition well suited to digital interfaces and children's content.
description: Fredoka's soft, rounded terminals give it an immediately welcoming character without tipping into novelty or excess. The inclusion of Hebrew alongside Latin makes it a rare and practical choice for bilingual digital products targeting both script communities. Its weight range from Light to Bold adds enough flexibility for use across interface hierarchies and light editorial work.

name: Freehand
classification_notes: Freehand is a single-weight display script based on traditional Khmer calligraphic forms, adapted to also support Latin. Its organic, brushed strokes make it a culturally specific and expressive choice for Khmer-language design.
summary: A calligraphic display script rooted in Khmer brush lettering traditions, offering an expressive option for Khmer and Latin typesetting.
description: Freehand is one of relatively few typefaces on Google Fonts to offer genuine support for the Khmer script, making it a valuable resource for Cambodian-language design projects. Its fluid, brush-drawn strokes carry a natural, handcrafted energy appropriate for posters, branding, and editorial display in both Khmer and Latin contexts. The single weight limits its flexibility, but its cultural specificity and calligraphic quality give it a clear and distinctive purpose.

name: Freeman
classification_notes: Freeman is a bold, single-weight display sans with strong geometric construction and confident letterforms. Its thick strokes and tight spacing give it a pronounced, assertive presence suited to large-scale use.
summary: A punchy, heavyweight display sans built for maximum impact at large sizes.
description: Freeman's bold geometric construction and uniform stroke weight make it well suited to headlines, posters, and branding work where visual authority is required. Its single Regular weight limits versatility, but within its intended role as a display face it performs with confidence. Best deployed at large sizes where its strong letterforms can assert themselves fully.

name: Fresca
classification_notes: Fresca is a clean, lightly spaced humanist sans-serif with open apertures and a somewhat neutral character. Its modest stroke contrast and upright stance make it readable at text sizes without drawing particular attention to itself.
summary: A quietly capable humanist sans that gets out of the way and lets the text breathe.
description: Fresca's open letterforms and balanced proportions make it a reasonable choice for body copy and interface text where legibility is the primary concern. It lacks the personality to anchor strong branding work but functions reliably in editorial and web contexts. Its single weight limits flexibility for typographic hierarchy.

name: Frijole
classification_notes: Frijole is a novelty display typeface with heavily textured, irregular letterforms evoking aged wood type or folk art lettering. Its exaggerated organic quality is entirely purposeful and leaves very little room for serious typographic application.
summary: A boldly textured novelty display face with strong folk-art and vintage street-food energy.
description: Frijole leans hard into a single aesthetic — rough, festive, and deliberately exuberant — making it a one-trick typeface with a very specific use case. It suits menus, event posters, or branding for casual food and entertainment contexts where a handmade, carnival-esque character is desirable. Outside that narrow brief, its usefulness drops off sharply.

name: Fruktur
classification_notes: Fruktur is an expressive blackletter-influenced display face with angular, fractured strokes and a distinctly Eastern European flavour reinforced by its Cyrillic Extended support. Its aggressive forms are firmly in decorative territory.
summary: A fractured blackletter display face with strong angular energy and notable Cyrillic support.
description: Fruktur occupies a niche intersection of blackletter tradition and expressive display design, with its sharp, broken letterforms lending themselves to poster work, album artwork, and editorial contexts with an edge. The inclusion of Cyrillic Extended script support is a genuine differentiator on Google Fonts. Its single weight and high stylisation restrict it to headline and decorative use only.

name: Fugaz One
classification_notes: Fugaz One is an upright italic-influenced display sans with strong diagonal stress and dynamic, energetic letterforms. The forward-leaning tension in its strokes gives it considerable momentum for headline work.
summary: An energetic display sans with built-in momentum, designed to read fast and hit hard.
description: Fugaz One's italicised construction gives its upright letterforms a sense of forward drive that makes it particularly effective for sports, entertainment, and promotional contexts. It is a single-weight face, which limits its utility in complex typographic systems, but as a standalone headline font it delivers real impact. Works best at large sizes where its dynamic character is fully legible.

name: Fuggles
classification_notes: Fuggles is a flowing, informal calligraphic script with natural pen-influenced strokes and a relaxed, unpretentious rhythm. Its letterforms suggest a broad-nib pen handled with ease rather than formal precision.
summary: A relaxed calligraphic script with genuine pen-drawn warmth and informal charm.
description: Fuggles strikes a comfortable balance between calligraphic tradition and approachable informality, making it well suited to packaging, branding, and editorial contexts that call for a handmade quality without formality. Its Latin Extended and Vietnamese support gives it broader linguistic reach than most script fonts of this type. The single Regular weight is appropriate given the script category, though it limits typographic range.

name: Funnel Display
classification_notes: Funnel Display is a large optical-size companion to Funnel Sans, designed with looser spacing and more pronounced geometric characteristics optimised for headline and display use. Its multi-weight range gives it real typographic utility.
summary: A considered geometric display sans with a full weight range designed specifically for large-scale setting.
description: Funnel Display is purpose-built for headlines and display contexts, with letterforms and spacing calibrated for optical performance at large sizes. Its range from Light to ExtraBold provides meaningful flexibility for editorial and branding hierarchies. The pairing relationship with Funnel Sans makes it a particularly practical choice for design systems that need both display and text weights from the same family.

name: Funnel Sans
classification_notes: Funnel Sans is a clean geometric sans-serif with text and UI-optimised spacing, designed as the workhorse companion to Funnel Display. Its multi-weight range and geometric rigour make it a capable system font.
summary: A clean, geometric sans designed for text and UI use, with a full weight range and a natural display companion.
description: Funnel Sans delivers geometric precision without sacrificing readability at smaller sizes, making it a solid choice for interface design, editorial body text, and branding systems. Its six weights from Light to ExtraBold provide genuine typographic flexibility for complex hierarchy. Used alongside Funnel Display, it forms a coherent two-opticals family that suits modern digital-first design workflows.

name: Fustat
classification_notes: Fustat is a contemporary Arabic-Latin typeface with clean, open letterforms that balance legibility across both scripts. Its humanist construction gives it warmth while maintaining neutrality suitable for extended reading.
summary: A well-considered Arabic-Latin humanist sans that achieves genuine harmony across two distinct writing systems.
description: Fustat's strength lies in its careful optical matching between Arabic and Latin scripts, making it genuinely useful for bilingual publishing and digital interfaces. Its generous weight range from ExtraLight to ExtraBold gives designers real flexibility for hierarchy. It suits editorial work, apps, and any context requiring clear, neutral typography in both Arabic and Latin.

name: Fuzzy Bubbles
classification_notes: Fuzzy Bubbles mimics a casual, rounded hand-lettering style with a deliberately childlike, bubbly quality. Strokes are thick and inflated, with minimal contrast and a playful irregularity.
summary: A cheerful bubble-letter script that leans entirely into its playful, childlike personality.
description: Fuzzy Bubbles is unambiguously a display face, best deployed at large sizes where its rounded, inflated strokes can make an impression. It suits children's content, casual branding, greeting cards, and social media graphics where a warm, approachable tone is paramount. Its Bold weight amplifies the effect considerably, making it the go-to choice for headlines in fun, informal contexts.

name: GFS Didot
classification_notes: GFS Didot is a revival of the classic Didot model adapted for the Greek script, featuring the characteristic extreme thick-thin contrast and hairline serifs of the Didone tradition. It is the definitive modern Greek serif of its kind.
summary: The authoritative Greek Didone serif, bringing sharp neoclassical elegance to the Greek script.
description: Developed by the Greek Font Society, GFS Didot applies the high-contrast Didot aesthetic — hairline serifs, dramatic stroke modulation — to Greek letterforms with genuine historical scholarship behind it. It is ideally suited to headlines, fashion editorial, luxury branding, and any context where classical refinement in Greek typography is required. Its single Regular weight limits its utility in text settings but makes it a compelling display choice.

name: GFS Neohellenic
classification_notes: GFS Neohellenic is a revival of a mid-twentieth-century Greek sans-serif with a distinctly scholarly, institutional character. Its letterforms are based on a typeface developed for academic publishing, giving it an authoritative yet approachable quality.
summary: A historically grounded Greek sans-serif revival with a clean academic character that reads well in text.
description: Originally developed for the Cambridge University Press in the early twentieth century, GFS Neohellenic was revived by the Greek Font Society and remains one of the most reliable Greek sans-serif options for scholarly and editorial work. Its Latin companion is simple and functional, making it a credible choice for bilingual Greek-Latin documents. The Regular and Bold weights cover the essential typographic needs of body copy and moderate headline use.

name: Ga Maamli
classification_notes: Ga Maamli is a highly decorative display typeface with distinctive cutout or inline stylistic features that give it a strong graphic personality. It is a one-weight novelty face with limited functional range.
summary: A distinctive decorative display face with strong graphic character suited to bold headline applications.
description: Ga Maamli makes a confident visual statement at large sizes, with its unusual letterform treatment giving it an immediately recognisable quality. It works best as a headline or logotype element in branding, packaging, or poster contexts where a single word or short phrase needs to command attention. Its single weight and narrow use case make it a specialised tool rather than a versatile workhorse.

name: Gabarito
classification_notes: Gabarito is a contemporary rounded display sans with soft, friendly terminals and a strong personality that scales from regular to black. Its construction is geometric with humanist touches, giving it energy without sacrificing legibility.
summary: A lively rounded sans with a broad weight range that brings approachable personality to branding and display work.
description: Gabarito's rounded terminals and confident proportions make it well suited to youth-oriented branding, app interfaces, and editorial headlines where warmth and energy are priorities. Its six weights from Regular to Black give designers genuine versatility for building typographic hierarchy within a single, cohesive voice. It occupies a useful space between purely geometric and expressive display type.

name: Gabriela
classification_notes: Gabriela is a text serif with old-style influences and a slightly ornamental quality, designed with Cyrillic script support alongside Latin. Its letterforms have a refined, traditional character with moderate stroke contrast.
summary: A quietly refined text serif with solid Cyrillic support, suited to long-form reading across multiple languages.
description: Gabriela offers a dependable option for publishers and designers working with Russian, Ukrainian, or other Cyrillic-script languages alongside Latin, where a consistent typographic voice across scripts is necessary. Its single Regular weight limits its usefulness for complex typographic hierarchy but makes it a straightforward choice for body text. The design is conservative enough to work in literary publishing, academic documents, and editorial contexts.

name: Gaegu
classification_notes: Gaegu is a Korean handwriting-style typeface that captures the informal, pen-written quality of casual Korean script alongside Latin. Its strokes are loose and natural, conveying an authentic handmade character.
summary: A natural Korean handwriting face that brings genuine informality and charm to bilingual Korean-Latin settings.
description: Gaegu is one of relatively few Korean handwriting typefaces available on Google Fonts, making it a valuable resource for designers working with Korean content who need an informal, personal tone. Its three weights — Light, Regular, and Bold — offer modest flexibility for headlines, captions, and short display text. It suits social media content, informal branding, children's materials, and any context where a hand-crafted Korean aesthetic is desirable.

name: Gafata
classification_notes: Gafata is a clean humanist sans-serif with open apertures and slightly organic letterforms. It has a quiet, understated character suited to extended reading at small sizes.
summary: A modest humanist sans with open forms that works quietly as a text face for digital use.
description: Gafata's open apertures and gentle humanist proportions give it legibility at small sizes without drawing attention to itself. It suits body copy in web and app interfaces where a neutral but warm tone is desired. Its Latin Extended coverage makes it useful for multilingual European projects.

name: Gajraj One
classification_notes: Gajraj One is a bold display typeface designed primarily for Devanagari script with matching Latin, featuring strong ink-trap-style cuts and a confident, modern aesthetic. The letterforms are robust and attention-grabbing, suited to headlines and branding.
summary: A bold, contemporary display face bridging Devanagari and Latin with strong, punchy letterforms.
description: Gajraj One's heavy weight and geometric confidence make it well suited to headlines and branding in Hindi and other Devanagari-script languages. Its Latin companion maintains a consistent visual weight, enabling cohesive bilingual typesetting. It works best at large sizes where its bold presence can make a clear statement.

name: Galada
classification_notes: Galada is an expressive display typeface designed for the Bengali script with a Latin companion, featuring decorative, calligraphically inspired strokes and high visual energy. It is built for large-scale, attention-grabbing applications rather than body text.
summary: An expressive Bengali display face with decorative calligraphic energy, built for bold headlines.
description: Galada draws on calligraphic traditions of the Bengali script to create a lively, distinctive display typeface. Its flowing strokes and decorative detailing give it a festive, vibrant character ideal for posters, event materials, and editorial headlines in Bengali-language contexts. The Latin companion maintains a complementary warmth, though the Bengali forms are clearly the primary focus.

name: Galdeano
classification_notes: Galdeano is a clean geometric sans-serif with rounded, even strokes and a friendly, approachable character. Its simplified letterforms sit somewhere between a pure geometric and a soft humanist style.
summary: A clean, lightly geometric sans with a friendly tone that suits interface and screen use.
description: Galdeano's even stroke widths and open counters give it a calm, approachable quality that works well in digital interfaces and light editorial settings. It lacks the full weight range needed for a true workhorse, but its single regular weight is pleasant and readable. Best deployed for UI copy, captions, or supplementary text rather than as a primary display face.

name: Galindo
classification_notes: Galindo is a bold display typeface with strong, chunky letterforms that evoke hand-lettered signage and Latin American graphic traditions. It has a rough, expressive character with a retro flavour.
summary: A chunky, retro-inflected display face inspired by hand-lettered Latin American signage traditions.
description: Galindo's heavy, irregular letterforms carry the energy of hand-painted shop signs and vintage poster lettering, making it well suited to branding projects that want a bold, characterful aesthetic. It works best at large sizes where its quirks read as personality rather than roughness. A strong choice for food and beverage branding, music events, or any project seeking a warm, populist feel.

name: Gamja Flower
classification_notes: Gamja Flower is a casual, hand-drawn script designed primarily for Korean (Hangul) with a matching Latin, featuring rounded, childlike strokes that feel informal and playful. It is optimised for friendly, expressive communication rather than legibility at small sizes.
summary: A cheerful hand-drawn Korean script face with rounded, childlike forms and a warm, informal personality.
description: Gamja Flower's soft, irregular strokes mimic natural handwriting in a way that feels approachable and light-hearted, making it popular for children's content, social media graphics, and informal branding. The Hangul letterforms are the primary draw, with the Latin companion maintaining the same casual energy. It is not suited to professional or formal applications but excels wherever warmth and informality are the goal.

name: Gantari
classification_notes: Gantari is a versatile geometric sans-serif with a full nine-weight range, featuring clean, rational letterforms with subtle humanist touches that aid legibility on screen. It is designed with modern UI and editorial applications clearly in mind.
summary: A well-constructed geometric sans with a broad weight range, built for both interface design and editorial use.
description: Gantari's nine weights from Thin to Black make it a genuinely flexible workhorse capable of handling everything from small UI copy to large display headlines within a single type family. Its geometric foundations are softened by subtle humanist details that maintain legibility across sizes and weights. It is a solid choice for product interfaces, digital publishing, and brand identity systems that require typographic consistency.

name: Gasoek One
classification_notes: Gasoek One is an extremely wide, heavy display sans-serif designed for Korean with Latin support, featuring bold, expanded letterforms that dominate the page with maximum visual impact. Its forms are clean and geometric, emphasising width over everything else.
summary: An ultra-wide, heavyweight display sans designed for Korean and Latin that commands attention at any scale.
description: Gasoek One pushes both the Hangul and Latin letterforms to extreme horizontal proportions, creating a typeface built entirely for impact at large sizes. Its clean, geometric construction keeps the expanded forms readable and controlled rather than chaotic, making it effective for posters, banners, and bold headline applications. There is essentially no use case for this font at body sizes — it lives entirely in the realm of display.

name: Gayathri
classification_notes: Gayathri is a clean, low-contrast sans-serif designed to serve both Malayalam and Latin scripts with equal elegance. Its forms are open and legible, with a warm humanist quality that suits text and interface use.
summary: A quietly capable dual-script sans-serif bridging Malayalam and Latin with consistent warmth and clarity.
description: Gayathri was developed specifically to provide a high-quality Malayalam typeface that pairs naturally with Latin text, making it particularly valuable for South Indian publishing, digital media, and multilingual interfaces. Its restrained design avoids novelty in favour of sustained readability. With Thin, Regular, and Bold weights available, it covers essential typographic needs without excess.

name: Geist
classification_notes: Geist is a clean, precise geometric sans-serif with strong Swiss influences, designed with developer tools and interfaces in mind. Its forms are neutral and rational, with consistent proportions and excellent screen rendering across a full nine-weight range.
summary: Vercel's purpose-built geometric sans-serif, refined for developer interfaces and digital products.
description: Geist was created by Vercel as the typographic identity of their development platform, and it shows — the design prioritises legibility at small sizes, crisp rendering on screen, and a neutral personality that recedes in favour of content. Its full weight range from Thin to Black makes it genuinely versatile for both UI and editorial applications. The Cyrillic support broadens its appeal considerably for international product teams.

name: Geist Mono
classification_notes: Geist Mono shares the rational geometry of its sans counterpart but adapts it for fixed-width contexts, maintaining consistent optical texture across all characters. It is designed with code editors and terminal interfaces as its primary environment.
summary: The monospaced companion to Geist, built for code editors and technical interfaces with the same geometric rigour.
description: Geist Mono extends Vercel's type system into fixed-width territory, providing a coding font that integrates seamlessly with Geist in mixed typographic environments. Character disambiguation is handled with care, ensuring numerals, letters, and punctuation remain distinct at small display sizes. Its nine weights — unusual for a monospace — give developers and designers genuine expressive range beyond the typical Regular and Bold pairing.

name: Gelasio
classification_notes: Gelasio is a metric-compatible companion to ITC Georgia, preserving its proportions and spacing while introducing its own refined details. It features moderate contrast, sturdy serifs, and excellent legibility characteristics for body text use.
summary: A metric-compatible serif designed as a refined alternative to Georgia, with strong body text credentials.
description: Gelasio was crafted to be interchangeable with Georgia in layout, making it a practical upgrade for documents and web pages that rely on that ubiquitous system serif. Its design carries more typographic character than Georgia while preserving the generous x-height and sturdy construction that made Georgia so enduring on screen. Vietnamese support alongside broad Latin coverage makes it a dependable choice for multilingual editorial and publishing work.

name: Gemunu Libre
classification_notes: Gemunu Libre is a dual-script sans-serif designed to harmonise Sinhala and Latin letterforms within a unified typographic system. Its forms are open and moderately geometric, with a friendly legibility suited to both text and display use.
summary: A thoughtfully constructed dual-script sans-serif uniting Sinhala and Latin in a coherent and legible system.
description: Gemunu Libre addresses a genuine gap in quality typefaces for the Sinhala script, providing a contemporary design that works fluently alongside Latin text without compromise to either script. The extended weight range from ExtraLight to ExtraBold gives it versatility across editorial, branding, and interface applications. It is especially well suited to Sri Lankan publishers, digital platforms, and organisations needing professional multilingual typography.

name: Genos
classification_notes: Genos is a wide, high-contrast geometric sans-serif with a distinctly display-oriented personality, featuring optically refined letterforms across a broad weight range. Its slightly flared stroke terminals and dramatic thin-to-thick transitions give it an expressive edge unusual in sans-serifs.
summary: A dramatic, high-contrast geometric sans-serif with display ambitions and a strikingly wide, elegant presence.
description: Genos occupies an interesting space between serif elegance and sans-serif rationalism, using contrast modulation and refined proportions to produce a typeface that feels simultaneously modern and classical. Its Cherokee script support is a notable and uncommon inclusion, reflecting genuine commitment to indigenous script coverage. Best deployed in headlines, editorial contexts, and branding where a confident, slightly unconventional sans is required.

name: Gentium Book Plus
classification_notes: Gentium Book Plus is the bolder, more text-optimised member of the Gentium family, designed for sustained reading in print and on screen with a slightly increased stroke weight. Its design draws on Renaissance humanist traditions, with graceful letterforms and extensive diacritic support.
summary: The text-weight variant of Gentium, offering Renaissance-inflected serif elegance with extraordinary script coverage.
description: Gentium Book Plus is engineered for environments where text density demands a slightly more robust stroke, making it preferable to Gentium Plus for body copy in print publications and long-form digital reading. Its extensive support for Cyrillic, Greek, and a vast range of Latin diacritics makes it a go-to resource for academic publishers, Bible societies, and linguists working across multiple languages. The design's humanist warmth ensures it never feels cold or mechanical despite its scholarly utility.

name: Gentium Plus
classification_notes: Gentium Plus is a graceful, Renaissance-inspired humanist serif with exceptional multilingual coverage, designed for scholarly and literary text setting. Its letterforms are elegant and carefully drawn, with particular attention to the consistency of diacritical marks across hundreds of characters.
summary: A distinguished humanist serif with unrivalled multilingual reach, built for serious scholarly and literary text work.
description: Gentium Plus originated from SIL International's mission to support minority language communities worldwide, resulting in one of the most extensively character-complete serif typefaces available under an open licence. Its Renaissance influences produce letterforms of genuine beauty that reward sustained reading in books, academic papers, and liturgical texts. The combination of typographic quality and breadth of script support makes it essentially unmatched among free serif typefaces for complex multilingual publishing.

name: Geo
classification_notes: A minimal geometric sans-serif with circular letterforms and very limited weight range. Designed for display use rather than extended reading.
summary: A stripped-back geometric sans built on near-perfect circles, offering a clean modernist aesthetic.
description: Geo's circular construction gives it a cold, precise character suited to tech branding and short display text. Its single weight limits versatility, making it a specialist tool rather than a workhorse. Best deployed for logos, headings, or interface elements where minimal warmth is an asset.

name: Geologica
classification_notes: A contemporary humanist sans with strong multilingual support across Latin, Cyrillic, and Greek scripts. Features optical corrections and a variable axis for flexible use.
summary: A versatile humanist sans with exceptional multilingual coverage and a full variable weight range.
description: Geologica covers Cyrillic Extended and Greek alongside Latin, making it a serious choice for international publishing and global digital products. Its humanist construction keeps text warm and readable at body sizes, while the nine-weight range and variable axis give designers precise control. Works equally well in editorial layouts, UI design, and long-form reading environments.

name: Geom
classification_notes: A geometric sans with multiple weights and broad script support including Greek, designed with clean rational forms. Balances modernity with functional legibility.
summary: A clean geometric sans offering solid multilingual support and a broad weight range for design flexibility.
description: Geom's rational geometric construction suits branding and headline work where precision and clarity are priorities. Its support for Greek alongside Latin Extended makes it useful for international projects. The seven-weight range provides enough variety for typographic hierarchy without a variable axis.

name: Georama
classification_notes: A wide-proportioned geometric sans with a full nine-weight range and extended Latin support. Letterforms favour open, airy spacing with slightly wider-than-average proportions.
summary: A wide-set geometric sans with generous proportions and a comprehensive weight range for versatile use.
description: Georama's notably wide proportions give layouts a spacious, open feel that suits editorial headlines and brand identities wanting approachability over rigidity. Its full weight range from Thin to Black makes it a flexible family for pairing across typographic hierarchies. Vietnamese support extends its usefulness for Southeast Asian markets.

name: Geostar
classification_notes: An ornamental display face built from geometric shapes with an inline or structural detail that gives letters a mechanical, architectural quality. Single weight severely limits application.
summary: A geometric display typeface with architectural, modular letterforms built from interlocking shapes.
description: Geostar constructs its letters from rigid geometric segments, giving the face a distinctive mechanical and almost technical character. Its novelty is its main appeal, making it suited to logos, posters, and headlines where a unique visual identity is needed. A single weight and limited character set mean it has a narrow range of practical use.

name: Geostar Fill
classification_notes: The filled companion to Geostar, sharing its modular geometric construction but with solid filled forms rather than outlined structures. Offers a bolder, more solid visual presence.
summary: The solid-filled counterpart to Geostar, delivering the same modular geometry with greater visual weight.
description: Geostar Fill shares the exact structure of Geostar but fills its forms, creating a denser, more impactful display option. The two can be paired for layered typographic effects, though both remain highly specialised tools. Practical use is limited to logotypes, posters, and short decorative headings.

name: Germania One
classification_notes: A bold, stylised display face drawing from Germanic blackletter and nationalist-era lettering traditions. Heavy strokes and pointed terminals give it an aggressive, historical character.
summary: A heavy blackletter-influenced display face with strong Germanic historical associations.
description: Germania One draws directly from early twentieth-century German lettering traditions, giving it an unmistakably archaic and forceful presence. Its associations are historically loaded, making context critical — it suits metal, rock, heritage, or deliberately retro-styled projects with care. A single weight and Latin-only support confirm its role as a narrow-purpose display tool.

name: Gideon Roman
classification_notes: An ornate display serif with high contrast, bracketed serifs, and decorative detailing reminiscent of Victorian and classical Roman lettering. Clearly intended for large-size headline use only.
summary: An ornate high-contrast display serif with Victorian flair and intricate decorative detailing.
description: Gideon Roman channels nineteenth-century display type with dramatic thick-thin contrast and embellished serifs that demand large sizes to read effectively. It suits editorial headlines, posters, and luxury packaging where a sense of period grandeur is the goal. Its extended Latin and Vietnamese support is a welcome practical touch for an otherwise highly decorative face.

name: Gidole
classification_notes: Gidole is a clean, open-source geometric sans inspired by DIN, with regularised proportions and a modern technical character. It supports Cyrillic and Greek alongside Latin, making it a versatile multilingual option.
summary: A refined geometric sans drawing on DIN's industrial heritage, built for screen readability across multiple scripts.
description: Gidole takes DIN as its starting point but softens and regularises the forms for contemporary digital use. Its multilingual support across Latin, Cyrillic, and Greek makes it genuinely useful for international projects. It suits UI design, technical documentation, and editorial work at smaller sizes.

name: Gidugu
classification_notes: Gidugu is a single-weight sans designed primarily for the Telugu script, with modest Latin support. The letterforms are simple and functional, prioritising legibility over stylistic distinction.
summary: A functional single-weight sans built around the Telugu script with basic Latin accompaniment.
description: Gidugu's primary purpose is to serve Telugu readers, offering clean and legible letterforms suited to body text on screen. Its Latin companion is unremarkable and largely utilitarian. The font's value lies squarely in its Telugu coverage rather than any distinctive typographic personality.

name: Gilda Display
classification_notes: Gilda Display is an elegant single-weight display serif with refined stroke contrast and classical proportions reminiscent of transitional typefaces. It has a restrained sophistication suited to editorial and fashion contexts.
summary: A graceful, high-contrast display serif with classical proportions and understated elegance.
description: Gilda Display draws on transitional serif conventions, balancing sharp hairlines against robust main strokes for a refined, composed appearance. It performs best at larger sizes where its contrast and fine details can be properly appreciated. It is well suited to fashion editorials, luxury branding, and magazine headlines.

name: Girassol
classification_notes: Girassol is a decorative display typeface with an ornamental, hand-crafted quality and distinctive inline or textured detail work. It has a singular, expressive character that limits its versatility.
summary: A decorative display typeface with ornamental charm best deployed sparingly at large sizes.
description: Girassol has a handcrafted, festive quality that makes it memorable in the right context, particularly for posters, event branding, and packaging. Its single weight and highly stylised forms mean it functions as a headline accent rather than a workhorse. Overuse quickly diminishes its impact.

name: Give You Glory
classification_notes: Give You Glory is a casual, informal handwritten script with loose, energetic letterforms that mimic natural brushpen writing. It has a spontaneous, personal quality typical of greeting-card lettering.
summary: A loose, informal brush script with the spontaneous energy of handwritten lettering.
description: Give You Glory captures the relaxed feel of casual handwriting with irregular, lively strokes that suggest an authentic human touch. It works well on greeting cards, informal branding, and social media graphics where personality matters more than precision. Its single weight limits systematic typographic use.

name: Glass Antiqua
classification_notes: Glass Antiqua is an unusual display typeface evoking aged or etched lettering with a semi-transparent, layered quality reminiscent of antique glass signage. It is highly decorative and distinctly one-note in application.
summary: A distinctive display typeface evoking antique etched glass with an aged, ornamental character.
description: Glass Antiqua draws visual inspiration from Victorian-era glass signage and engraved lettering, giving it a nostalgic, atmospheric quality. It is best used at display sizes for posters, labels, and heritage branding where its period character is an asset. Its highly specialised aesthetic makes it impractical for general typesetting.

name: Glegoo
classification_notes: Glegoo is a sturdy, legible serif designed with Devanagari support alongside Latin, featuring moderate contrast and robust proportions suited to body text. Its paired scripts are carefully balanced for consistent reading rhythm.
summary: A reliable bilingual serif covering Devanagari and Latin, built for comfortable reading at text sizes.
description: Glegoo prioritises legibility and even texture across its Devanagari and Latin character sets, making it a practical choice for multilingual publishing in Hindi and other Devanagari-script languages alongside English. The two weights provide enough range for straightforward document hierarchy. It lacks flair but delivers consistent, dependable performance as a text face.

name: Gloock
classification_notes: Gloock is a high-contrast editorial serif with sharp, crisp details and strong vertical stress, designed for impactful display use. It has a contemporary editorial quality that feels indebted to modern high-fashion typography.
summary: A sharp, high-contrast editorial serif designed to command attention at display sizes.
description: Gloock brings a modern editorial sensibility to the high-contrast serif genre, with crisp hairlines and confident strokes that photograph and render well at large sizes. It is particularly well suited to magazine headlines, fashion branding, and premium digital editorial contexts. Its Cyrillic Extended support broadens its appeal for international editorial work.

name: Gloria Hallelujah
classification_notes: A hand-lettered script with a loose, bouncy baseline and irregular letterforms that mimic enthusiastic handwriting. The chunky strokes and exaggerated letterforms give it a distinctly playful, childlike energy.
summary: A boisterous hand-lettered script that channels the energy of felt-tip marker handwriting with deliberate imperfection.
description: Gloria Hallelujah is ideal for children's content, greeting cards, and casual social media graphics where an informal, enthusiastic tone is needed. Its irregular baseline and chunky strokes make it highly legible at display sizes despite its loose construction. It works best sparingly, as large blocks of text would fatigue the eye quickly.

name: Glory
classification_notes: A clean humanist sans-serif with open apertures and friendly proportions across a well-spaced weight range. The letterforms show subtle calligraphic influence with gentle stroke modulation that keeps it warm rather than clinical.
summary: A versatile humanist sans-serif with a warm, approachable character and a broad weight range suited to interface and editorial use.
description: Glory's open construction and friendly proportions make it a reliable choice for UI design, app interfaces, and web body text where legibility at small sizes is essential. The extensive weight range from Thin to ExtraBold provides strong typographic hierarchy. Vietnamese support alongside Latin Extended broadens its utility for multilingual projects.

name: Gluten
classification_notes: A soft, rounded display typeface with a variable-style axis that allows significant adjustment of its characteristic roundness and weight. The terminals are fully rounded and the overall construction is bubbly and expressive with a deliberately playful structure.
summary: A highly distinctive rounded display face with a variable axis that lets designers dial up or down its bubbly, inflated character.
description: Gluten's variable nature makes it unusually flexible for a display typeface, allowing fine-tuned control over its soft, rounded aesthetic. It suits branding, packaging, and headline work aimed at younger audiences or brands with a playful, approachable identity. Vietnamese and Latin Extended support makes it usable across a wider range of multilingual projects than most novelty display faces.

name: Goblin One
classification_notes: A heavy display face with bold, blocky letterforms that incorporate strong slab-like serifs and a dense, imposing structure reminiscent of mid-century woodblock printing. The single Regular weight limits its versatility considerably.
summary: A dense, heavyweight display serif with woodblock-inspired construction built for bold, attention-grabbing headlines.
description: Goblin One draws on the tradition of Victorian and mid-century poster typography, making it well suited to event posters, Halloween-adjacent branding, and bold editorial headlines. The single weight limits its flexibility, meaning it functions best as a standalone display element rather than part of a broader typographic system. It carries a strong personality that will dominate any layout it appears in.

name: Gochi Hand
classification_notes: A relaxed, informal handwriting script with rounded letterforms and a consistent, legible structure that avoids the extreme irregularity of some casual scripts. The stroke weight is relatively even, giving it an approachable, everyday quality.
summary: A cheerful, rounded handwriting script with enough consistency to remain legible while retaining a genuinely casual, hand-drawn feel.
description: Gochi Hand works well for educational content, informal web headings, and personal project branding where a hand-drawn aesthetic is desired without sacrificing clarity. Its relatively even stroke weight makes it more legible than many competing casual scripts at moderate sizes. It pairs naturally with clean, simple sans-serifs that contrast its informal warmth.

name: Goldman
classification_notes: A bold geometric display typeface with strong angular construction and high-contrast strokes that give it a sharp, modern edge. The design has a distinctly technical and premium character with precise, almost mechanical letterform geometry.
summary: A sharp, angular geometric display face with high-contrast strokes that projects a premium, contemporary authority.
description: Goldman suits luxury branding, fashion editorial, and technology product packaging where a confident, modern aesthetic is required. The two-weight range of Regular and Bold offers limited flexibility, positioning it primarily as a display and headline face rather than a workhorse for extended text. Its angular geometry and strong contrast make it particularly effective for logotype work and short impactful headlines.

name: Golos Text
classification_notes: A humanist sans-serif designed with strong Cyrillic support alongside Latin, featuring clean, legible letterforms with good optical balance across its weight range. The design prioritises text performance with open counters and careful spacing that works well at body text sizes.
summary: A well-crafted humanist sans-serif with excellent Cyrillic support, built with the rigour needed for reliable body text performance.
description: Golos Text was developed with Russian-language publishing and digital interfaces in mind, making it an outstanding choice for Cyrillic-heavy projects that require a modern, neutral typeface. Its clean, open construction translates well to screen use at a range of sizes, from UI labels to longer editorial text. The range from Regular to Black provides workable typographic hierarchy for complex documents and interfaces.

name: Google Sans
classification_notes: A clean geometric sans-serif with softened terminals and a highly legible construction optimised for screen and product interfaces. The design reflects Google's Material Design system with precise, neutral letterforms and broad multilingual script support including Armenian and Bengali.
summary: Google's own geometric sans-serif, engineered for legibility across diverse scripts and optimised for product and interface use at all sizes.
description: Google Sans is a system typeface built to function across Google's product ecosystem, with thoughtful support for Armenian, Bengali, and Canadian Aboriginal syllabics alongside Latin. Its clean geometric construction and neutral character ensure it does not impose personality on functional interfaces, instead prioritising clarity and consistency. While strong in UI contexts, its system origins mean it carries limited expressive range for editorial or branding work.

name: Google Sans Code
classification_notes: A monospaced variant of the Google Sans family, designed specifically for coding environments with clear glyph differentiation and multiple weights for hierarchy. Its broad script support, including Adlam and Canadian Aboriginal syllabics, is unusually extensive for a coding font.
summary: Google's own coding typeface, distinguished by its extensive script support and clean, legible forms across a generous weight range.
description: Google Sans Code brings the geometric clarity of the Google Sans family to the coding context, with fixed-width letterforms optimised for readability on screen. Its support for Adlam, Canadian Aboriginal, and Cherokee scripts sets it apart from most coding fonts. Suitable for IDEs, code editors, and technical documentation where multilingual support is a genuine requirement.

name: Google Sans Flex
classification_notes: A highly flexible variable font expanding on the Google Sans design language, offering a sweeping nine-weight range and broad script support including Canadian Aboriginal and Cherokee. Its variable nature makes it exceptionally versatile across digital product contexts.
summary: An expansive variable extension of the Google Sans family with a wide weight range and rare indigenous script support.
description: Google Sans Flex stretches the familiar Google Sans geometry across nine static weights and a variable axis, giving designers fine-grained control over typographic tone. The inclusion of Canadian Aboriginal and Cherokee scripts makes it a thoughtful choice for inclusive digital products. Its clean, neutral character suits interface design, dashboards, and brand communications.

name: Gorditas
classification_notes: A chunky, exuberant display face with heavily rounded terminals and a distinctly playful, cartoon-inflected personality. Available in only two weights, limiting its typographic versatility considerably.
summary: A boisterous rounded display face with cartoon energy, best deployed sparingly for titles and playful branding.
description: Gorditas leans hard into its soft, inflated letterforms, making an immediate impression in headline and poster contexts. Its limited two-weight range — Regular and Bold — constrains its utility, but within that scope it delivers genuine character. It suits children's publishing, food packaging, and any project requiring warmth and approachability over refinement.

name: Gothic A1
classification_notes: A clean, neutral grotesque optimised primarily for Korean Hangul, with a full nine-weight range providing excellent typographic range. Its Latin characters are functional and harmonious with the Hangul forms without being particularly distinctive.
summary: A dependable, wide-ranging Korean grotesque that covers everything from thin editorial weights to heavy display use.
description: Gothic A1 is a thoroughly considered sans-serif built around the needs of Korean text, with Hangul letterforms that remain clear and balanced at small sizes. The nine-weight progression gives it genuine versatility across editorial, interface, and branding applications. Latin, Cyrillic, and Greek support is solid, making it a practical workhorse for multilingual Korean-primary projects.

name: Gotu
classification_notes: A single-weight sans-serif with Devanagari support, exhibiting quiet, even proportions suited to extended reading in both Latin and Devanagari scripts. Its restraint and lack of additional weights limits its typographic flexibility.
summary: A calm, unassuming sans-serif covering Devanagari and Latin with readable proportions and a modest single-weight offering.
description: Gotu prioritises legibility and script harmony over personality, making it a straightforward choice for bilingual Devanagari-Latin text at body sizes. Its even stroke weight and open letterforms hold up well on screen. The absence of additional weights means it functions best in content-heavy contexts where a single text weight suffices.

name: Goudy Bookletter 1911
classification_notes: A revival of Frederic Goudy's Kennerley Old Style typeface as it appeared in the 1911 printing of 'The Chap-Book', featuring characterful old-style proportions, moderate contrast, and distinctly antiquarian detailing. Available in a single weight with Latin support only.
summary: A faithful revival of Goudy's early twentieth-century old-style, brimming with historical character and craft.
description: Goudy Bookletter 1911 reproduces the warm, slightly irregular quality of metal type as it appeared in a specific 1911 publication, giving it an authenticity that distinguishes it from more polished revivals. Its old-style proportions and moderate contrast make it suitable for literary editorial work, book covers, and heritage branding. The single weight limits functional range but keeps its application purposeful.

name: Gowun Batang
classification_notes: A Korean Batang-style serif with clean, upright letterforms and a harmonious Latin companion, covering a broad Korean character set. Its two-weight range — Regular and Bold — provides minimal but functional typographic variation.
summary: A clean Korean Batang serif with an honest design and solid coverage of Hangul for digital reading.
description: Gowun Batang follows the conventions of the traditional Korean Batang serif style, with structured strokes and clear contrast suited to longer-form reading. Its Latin Extended support ensures it performs well in bilingual documents and web publishing. The limited weight range suits projects where typographic hierarchy is handled through size and layout rather than weight variation.

name: Gowun Dodum
classification_notes: A straightforward Korean Dodum-style sans-serif providing uniform stroke weight and open letterforms for comfortable on-screen reading. Single-weight only, restricting its scope to body and UI text rather than hierarchical typography.
summary: A plain, dependable Korean gothic sans-serif designed for readable on-screen text in a single functional weight.
description: Gowun Dodum delivers the essential qualities of a Dodum-style Korean sans — even stroke weight, clear Hangul construction, and good screen legibility — without ornamentation.
