Its single Regular weight makes it best suited to body copy, captions, and interface labels where consistency rather than contrast is the priority. Paired with Gowun Batang, it forms a coherent Korean type system.

name: Graduate
classification_notes: Graduate is an all-caps serif with a distinct collegiate, varsity feel, drawing on American block letter traditions. It features thick strokes, bracketed serifs, and an athletic aesthetic suited to display applications.
summary: A robust all-caps collegiate serif that channels American varsity and sports typography with considerable authority.
description: Graduate draws directly from the tradition of American university and athletic lettering, making it immediately recognisable in sports, gaming, and school-themed branding contexts. Its heavy bracketed serifs and uniform capitalisation give it a commanding presence at large sizes. It is best reserved for headlines, logos, and display settings where its retro sporting character can be fully exploited.

name: Grand Hotel
classification_notes: Grand Hotel is a connected script with an Art Deco sensibility, featuring elegant, flowing letterforms with moderate contrast and decorative flourishes. It balances legibility with period charm.
summary: An elegant Art Deco connected script that evokes the glamour and sophistication of the 1920s and 1930s.
description: Grand Hotel captures the refined luxury of interwar graphic design, with smooth connecting strokes and a confident, upright slant that reads as polished rather than casual. It suits branding, packaging, and editorial work where a sense of heritage and glamour is desirable. Its extended Latin character support makes it reasonably versatile across European languages.

name: Grandiflora One
classification_notes: Grandiflora One is a delicate, high-contrast serif with fine hairline strokes and pronounced stroke modulation, drawing on classical display type traditions. Its Korean and Latin support is carefully harmonised for bilingual typographic work.
summary: A refined high-contrast display serif notable for its rare and well-executed integration of Korean and Latin scripts.
description: Grandiflora One stands out on Google Fonts for its genuine bilingual design ambition, offering harmonised Korean Hangul and Latin letterforms within a single elegant typeface. The fine hairlines and strong stroke contrast give it a luxurious, editorial quality well suited to fashion, beauty, and cultural branding. It is strictly a display face — the delicate thin strokes demand large sizes and quality rendering to make their mark.

name: Grandstander
classification_notes: Grandstander is a geometric, rounded sans-serif with a playful, informal character and a full range of weights from Thin to Black. Its consistent stroke terminals and friendly proportions give it a distinctive, approachable voice.
summary: A cheerful, rounded geometric sans with a wide weight range that brings warmth and personality to display and branding work.
description: Grandstander pairs the structure of geometric sans-serif design with generously rounded terminals, producing a typeface that feels energetic and approachable rather than cold or clinical. The nine weights provide considerable flexibility for hierarchy and emphasis within a consistent visual voice. It is well suited to children's products, app branding, editorial headlines, and any context that calls for friendly confidence.

name: Grape Nuts
classification_notes: Grape Nuts is a loose, informal handwritten script with an organic, irregular baseline and natural pen-like strokes. It has a distinctly casual, personal quality that mimics everyday handwriting rather than formal calligraphy.
summary: A loose, organic handwritten script with a genuine casual charm that mimics spontaneous everyday penmanship.
description: Grape Nuts eschews the polish of formal calligraphic scripts in favour of an endearingly imperfect, natural handwriting aesthetic. The irregular letterforms and relaxed connections give it authenticity and warmth, making it a reasonable choice for informal branding, artisanal packaging, and personal project headlines. Its use should be confined to display sizes, as the loose construction limits legibility at small scales.

name: Gravitas One
classification_notes: Gravitas One is a heavy, high-contrast display serif with pronounced slab-like elements and bold, commanding proportions. Its single regular weight is optimised for impact at large sizes.
summary: A heavyweight display slab serif built purely for impact, commanding attention in headlines and poster applications.
description: Gravitas One delivers exactly what its name promises — a dense, authoritative presence that dominates at display sizes through its bold weight and strong stroke contrast. The thick slabs and tight spacing create a sense of compressed energy suited to editorial headlines, advertising, and poster work. It is a one-trick face, but it performs that trick with conviction.

name: Great Vibes
classification_notes: Great Vibes is a flowing, high-quality formal cursive script with elegant, calligraphic letterforms and generous swash connections. Its Cyrillic and Greek support is notably broad for a script face.
summary: A graceful formal cursive script with sophisticated calligraphic flow and unusually broad multilingual script support.
description: Great Vibes is among the more accomplished formal script typefaces on Google Fonts, offering smooth, well-spaced connections and genuine calligraphic rhythm across its letterforms. Its support for Cyrillic and Greek scripts makes it a rare choice for formal multilingual design work such as wedding stationery, luxury branding, and editorial headers. The single weight is a limitation, but within its register it performs with elegance and consistency.

name: Grechen Fuemen
classification_notes: Grechen Fuemen is an informal, historical-style cursive script with an irregular, hand-drawn character evoking early modern European penmanship. Its rough texture and idiosyncratic forms give it a distinctly archaic personality.
summary: An idiosyncratic historical cursive script that channels the rough energy of early modern European hand lettering.
description: Grechen Fuemen occupies an unusual niche, drawing on historical cursive traditions to produce a typeface with genuine archaic character and textural interest. Its irregular strokes and quirky letterforms make it a strong choice for historical, craft, or folk-themed projects where conventional scripts would feel too polished. Legibility is limited at small sizes, and its highly specific aesthetic means its applications are genuinely narrow.

name: Grenze
classification_notes: Grenze is a high-contrast serif with a dark, gothic editorial character, drawing on blackletter influences while remaining rooted in Roman serif structure. Its dramatic stroke contrast and sharp serifs make it better suited to display sizes than continuous text.
summary: A high-contrast serif with gothic undertones, suited to dramatic editorial and display work.
description: Grenze occupies an unusual space between classical serif typography and blackletter tradition, with pronounced stroke contrast and sharply cut serifs that give it a brooding authority. It works well for editorial headlines, book covers, and branding projects that demand historical gravitas with a contemporary edge. Its wide weight range, from Thin to Black, gives designers flexibility across both refined and intense applications.

name: Grenze Gotisch
classification_notes: Grenze Gotisch is a contemporary blackletter with a variable-grade weight axis and refined proportions that soften some of the genre's traditional severity. It draws directly on Gothic textura traditions while maintaining legibility at display sizes.
summary: A refined variable blackletter that modernises the Gothic textura tradition for contemporary display use.
description: Grenze Gotisch brings genuine craft to the blackletter genre, offering a full nine-weight range that spans from ethereal Thin to commanding Black. Its letterforms are rooted in historical textura models but are drawn with enough openness to remain accessible in branding and editorial contexts. It suits music packaging, fashion editorial, and any project seeking a dark, historically charged atmosphere.

name: Grey Qo
classification_notes: Grey Qo is a flowing, thin connecting script with a delicate calligraphic quality, featuring smooth baseline connections and elegant letter proportions. Its single Regular weight limits versatility but the consistency of stroke weight gives it a refined, understated character.
summary: A delicate, thin connecting script with a refined calligraphic elegance suited to upmarket branding.
description: Grey Qo draws on pointed-pen calligraphic tradition, delivering smooth flowing connections between letters with a consistent, fine stroke weight. Its restraint makes it well suited to wedding stationery, luxury packaging, cosmetics branding, and fashion editorial where a light, sophisticated handwritten feel is required. The single weight limits adaptability, but within its niche it performs with genuine grace.

name: Griffy
classification_notes: Griffy is a rough, hand-drawn decorative display face with an irregular, scratchy texture that evokes hand-lettering on chalkboards or rustic signage. Its single weight and highly specific aesthetic sharply limit its application range.
summary: A rough hand-drawn display face with a scratchy, rustic character best used sparingly for novelty effect.
description: Griffy mimics the look of informal hand-lettering with deliberately uneven strokes and a textured, imperfect finish that suggests chalk or dry-brush work. It can work for themed menus, rustic branding, or retro poster design where an artisan, handcrafted feel is the explicit goal. Its one-trick nature and single weight make it a narrow choice, however charming it may be in the right context.

name: Gruppo
classification_notes: Gruppo is an ultra-low x-height geometric sans with wide, condensed-feeling proportions and a strongly stylised character rooted in 1920s–30s modernist lettering. Its extreme design choices make it highly distinctive but limited in functional range.
summary: An unusually proportioned geometric sans with a tiny x-height and art deco modernist character.
description: Gruppo's defining feature is its dramatically reduced x-height relative to its capitals, creating a distinctive rhythm that feels directly influenced by early twentieth-century modernist and art deco titling. It works as a headline or logotype face for projects where a period-inflected, architectural quality is desired. Its single weight and idiosyncratic proportions mean it functions best as an accent typeface rather than a workhorse.

name: Gudea
classification_notes: Gudea is a clean, neutral humanist sans-serif with open apertures and even stroke weights designed specifically for screen legibility. Its modest character set and two-weight range keep it practical but limited.
summary: A clean, screen-optimised humanist sans designed for comfortable reading in digital interfaces.
description: Gudea prioritises legibility above personality, with open counters, humanist letter construction, and even stroke contrast that performs reliably at small sizes on screen. It suits UI text, web body copy, and app interfaces where readability and neutrality take precedence over typographic distinction. The limited two-weight family constrains its typographic range, but both weights are well drawn and functionally sound.

name: Gugi
classification_notes: Gugi is a Korean-Latin display typeface with a geometric, modular construction that gives both scripts a consistent angular rhythm. Its single weight and display-focused design make it suited to headline use rather than extended text.
summary: A geometric modular display face harmonising Korean Hangul and Latin scripts with a consistent angular character.
description: Gugi's design challenge is achieving visual unity across Korean and Latin writing systems, which it accomplishes through a shared geometric, block-like construction applied to both scripts. It reads as contemporary and slightly tech-inflected, making it appropriate for Korean-language branding, game interfaces, and digital headline use. The single Regular weight limits its flexibility, but the cross-script consistency is genuinely well handled.

name: Gulzar
classification_notes: Gulzar is a Nastaliq-style Arabic typeface with sophisticated calligraphic letterforms following the flowing, diagonal baseline characteristic of this script tradition, paired with a complementary Latin. It is designed for high-quality text setting of Urdu and related languages.
summary: A refined Nastaliq Arabic typeface built for high-quality Urdu text setting with genuine calligraphic fidelity.
description: Gulzar addresses one of the more technically demanding areas of type design, implementing the complex glyph-positioning and diagonal baseline flow of Nastaliq script with care and authenticity. It is primarily intended for typesetting Urdu poetry and literary text, where the aesthetic conventions of Nastaliq calligraphy carry cultural and aesthetic weight. The inclusion of a Latin companion makes it usable in bilingual publishing contexts.

name: Gupter
classification_notes: Gupter is a refined serif with classical proportions, moderate contrast, and bracketed serifs reminiscent of 18th-century book typefaces. Its three weights make it practical for long-form editorial use.
summary: A classical transitional serif with restrained elegance suited to extended reading.
description: Gupter draws on the traditions of 18th-century European book typography, with moderate stroke contrast and sturdy bracketed serifs that hold up well at text sizes. Its three-weight range — Regular, Medium, and Bold — offers enough flexibility for editorial hierarchies. It works well in long-form print or screen reading environments where legibility and quiet authority are required.

name: Gurajada
classification_notes: Gurajada is a single-weight sans-serif designed primarily for the Telugu script, with a clean humanist approach that balances the complex curves of Telugu with a neutral Latin companion. Its design prioritises legibility over personality.
summary: A clean, legibility-focused sans-serif built around the Telugu script with a matching Latin.
description: Gurajada addresses the practical challenge of typesetting Telugu on screen, where the script's elaborate conjuncts and vowel marks demand careful spacing and clear stroke definition. The Latin companion follows a plain humanist model, keeping the two scripts visually harmonious. Its single weight limits its range, but it serves body text applications in Telugu-language digital publishing reliably.

name: Gveret Levin
classification_notes: Gveret Levin is an expressive, informal Hebrew script with a handwritten, marker-pen quality, offering a casual and characterful approach to Hebrew display typography. The Latin characters follow a similarly loose, informal style.
summary: An expressive, handwritten Hebrew script with an informal marker-pen character ideal for display use.
description: Gveret Levin brings a loose, spontaneous energy to Hebrew typography, evoking hand-lettering with a broad-tipped marker rather than a formal calligraphic pen. Its boldly casual forms make it well suited to headlines, packaging, and branding contexts where a warm, approachable tone is needed. The Latin complement maintains the same informal register, making it usable in bilingual Hebrew-Latin layouts.

name: Gwendolyn
classification_notes: Gwendolyn is a flowing, formal-leaning script with connected letterforms, generous swash details, and a light calligraphic influence that suits elegant display applications. The Bold weight adds presence for larger headline use.
summary: A flowing, elegant script with calligraphic roots suited to formal branding and display contexts.
description: Gwendolyn delivers the graceful, connected letterforms associated with formal penmanship, with enough refinement to work across wedding stationery, luxury packaging, and editorial headlines. Its swash details and smooth joins give it a genuinely handcrafted quality. The inclusion of a Bold weight and broad Latin Extended and Vietnamese coverage makes it more versatile than most display scripts of its kind.

name: Habibi
classification_notes: Habibi is a single-weight serif designed with Arabic script sensitivity in mind, though its Google Fonts offering covers Latin; it features warm, humanist proportions with moderate contrast and an understated elegance. It reads quietly at text sizes.
summary: A quietly elegant humanist serif with warm proportions and a calm, literary character.
description: Habibi takes a restrained approach to serif design, with humanist stroke modulation and moderate contrast that give it an approachable, literary feel rather than anything cold or mechanical. Its single weight limits typographic range, but within that constraint it performs well for body text in editorial and cultural publishing contexts. The name and sensibility suggest a design informed by cross-cultural typographic dialogue.

name: Hachi Maru Pop
classification_notes: Hachi Maru Pop is a rounded, bubbly Japanese script with a childlike, playful character, designed to evoke handwritten pop lettering used in Japanese stationery and casual communication. Its forms are uniformly rounded and soft.
summary: A bubbly, rounded Japanese script radiating cheerful, childlike energy from every letterform.
description: Hachi Maru Pop draws directly from the tradition of Japanese 'pop' handwriting — a rounded, balloon-like style associated with stationery, school notebooks, and youth culture. Every character, whether kanji, kana, Cyrillic, or Latin, shares the same soft, inflated quality, ensuring visual consistency across scripts. It suits packaging, social media graphics, children's content, and any context demanding an unambiguously upbeat, approachable tone.

name: Hahmlet
classification_notes: Hahmlet is a broad-family Korean serif with nine weights, combining classical serif structure with the demands of Hangul's geometric syllabic blocks, resulting in a versatile and well-crafted workhorse. Its wide weight range makes it genuinely functional across editorial hierarchies.
summary: A well-crafted Korean serif spanning nine weights that works equally hard in Hangul and Latin settings.
description: Hahmlet stands out among Korean Google Fonts for the ambition of its weight range, running from Thin to Black and giving designers proper control over typographic hierarchy in Korean-language contexts. The serif structure adapts thoughtfully to Hangul's syllabic geometry while maintaining a warm, humanist quality in both Korean and Latin character sets. It is a reliable choice for long-form digital publishing, editorial design, and any project requiring serious Korean typography.

name: Halant
classification_notes: Halant is a multi-weight serif designed for both Devanagari and Latin, with a humanist warmth and moderate contrast that makes it comfortable for extended reading in both scripts. Its five weights and dual-script design reflect careful cross-cultural typographic thinking.
summary: A thoughtfully designed dual-script serif balancing Devanagari and Latin with humanist warmth across five weights.
description: Halant was built with genuine care for the Devanagari script, ensuring that its calligraphic rhythm and headline strokes are handled with the same attention given to the Latin companion. The moderate stroke contrast and bracketed serifs make it comfortable for body text in both scripts, while the five-weight range supports proper editorial hierarchy. It is an excellent choice for bilingual Indian publishing, long-form web content, and academic contexts requiring reliable Devanagari support.

name: Hammersmith One
classification_notes: Hammersmith One features sturdy, wide letterforms with geometric underpinning and consistent stroke weight. Its single-weight design is optimised for headline and display use at larger sizes.
summary: A confident, wide geometric sans built for headlines and display settings where bold clarity is required.
description: Hammersmith One's broad proportions and even stroke weight give it a commanding presence on screen and in print. It works well for headlines, logotypes, and short bursts of display copy where legibility at a glance matters. Its single Regular weight keeps things focused, making it best paired with a contrasting body typeface.

name: Hanalei
classification_notes: Hanalei is a decorative display face with an inline treatment applied to bold, Art Deco-influenced letterforms. The open inline strokes give it a vintage, tropical resort quality.
summary: A bold Art Deco inline display face with a distinctly tropical, vintage resort character.
description: Hanalei draws on 1930s Art Deco lettering traditions, pairing chunky uppercase forms with decorative inline strokes that evoke signage from that era. It suits headline applications in branding, poster work, and packaging where a retro or exotic mood is desired. Hanalei Fill provides a companion solid version for layering effects.

name: Hanalei Fill
classification_notes: Hanalei Fill is the solid companion to Hanalei, sharing the same Art Deco letterform structure but without the inline cuts, enabling layered typographic effects when combined with its counterpart.
summary: The solid-fill companion to Hanalei, designed for layering with the inline version to create vintage two-tone display effects.
description: Hanalei Fill reproduces the same bold, Art Deco-inspired letterforms as Hanalei but renders them as solid shapes rather than inline outlines. When superimposed with Hanalei at the same size, the two fonts create a classic layered sign-painting effect. Its use cases mirror those of Hanalei — posters, packaging, and retro-themed branding.

name: Handjet
classification_notes: Handjet is an unusual modular typeface built from a grid of dot or stamp-like elements that simulate the output of a handheld label printer or dot-matrix device. It spans a wide variable weight range and supports multiple scripts including Arabic, Armenian, and Cyrillic.
summary: A variable modular typeface that mimics dot-matrix label-printer output across a wide weight range and multiple scripts.
description: Handjet's letterforms are constructed from a repeating grid of small stamp-like marks, evoking handheld label makers and industrial dot-matrix printing. Its variable axis covers Thin through Black, offering considerable flexibility for display and headline contexts. Multilingual support across Arabic, Armenian, and Cyrillic scripts makes it an unusually versatile choice for expressive international projects.

name: Handlee
classification_notes: Handlee is a loose, informal handwritten script based on the designer's own handwriting, with slightly irregular letterforms that give it an authentic pen-on-paper quality. It is single-weight and best suited to short, friendly copy.
summary: A relaxed, informal handwriting-style font that brings an authentic personal touch to short display copy.
description: Handlee mimics the natural inconsistencies of everyday handwriting, lending a warm and approachable character to greetings, invitations, and informal branding. Its single Regular weight keeps the offering simple, and its legibility is adequate at medium to large sizes though it degrades at small text settings. Best used sparingly for headlines, pull quotes, or short decorative lines rather than extended body copy.

name: Hanken Grotesk
classification_notes: Hanken Grotesk is a clean, contemporary grotesque with refined spacing and subtle humanist touches that ease readability across both screen and print contexts. Its extensive weight range and Cyrillic Extended support broaden its utility considerably.
summary: A refined contemporary grotesque that balances Swiss neutrality with subtle warmth, making it a reliable workhorse across UI, editorial, and branding contexts.
description: Hanken Grotesk draws on the neo-grotesque tradition whilst introducing slightly humanist details that prevent it from feeling cold or mechanical. Its nine-weight range — from Thin to Black — covers everything from delicate body text to bold headline work, and its Cyrillic Extended support extends its reach into multilingual projects. Clean spacing and open apertures make it a dependable choice for interfaces, long-form editorial, and corporate identity.

name: Hanuman
classification_notes: Hanuman is a serif typeface designed primarily for the Khmer script, with accompanying Latin glyphs, spanning a comprehensive nine-weight range. Its stroke modulation and serifs adapt the conventions of Western text typography sensitively to Khmer letterforms.
summary: A comprehensive Khmer serif typeface with matching Latin support, covering nine weights for versatile text and display use.
description: Hanuman addresses the need for high-quality, weight-varied serif typography in the Khmer script, a space historically underserved by digital type. Its forms balance traditional Khmer calligraphic conventions with the demands of on-screen legibility, making it suitable for editorial, publishing, and government or educational materials. The accompanying Latin glyphs are workmanlike and complement the Khmer at text sizes.

name: Happy Monkey
classification_notes: Happy Monkey is a quirky, rounded display face with exaggerated, playful letterforms and soft terminals that give it a friendly, informal character. It is a single-weight font suited to light-hearted display applications.
summary: A cheerful, rounded display typeface with exaggerated forms and soft terminals that project a playful, approachable personality.
description: Happy Monkey's bouncy proportions and rounded strokes make it well suited to children's content, casual branding, and light-hearted web headlines where a fun tone is needed. Its single weight limits flexibility but keeps the personality strong and consistent. It is best deployed at larger sizes where its distinctive quirks can be fully appreciated rather than lost.

name: Harmattan
classification_notes: Harmattan is designed specifically for Arabic script with a Sudanese regional style, featuring open, legible letterforms suited to extended reading. The Latin companion is clean and unobtrusive, supporting bilingual typesetting.
summary: A humanist sans designed primarily for Arabic script, with a regional Sudanese character and strong legibility at small sizes.
description: Harmattan supports Arabic and Latin in a single coherent typeface, making it a practical choice for multilingual documents and web content. Its open letterforms and moderate stroke contrast aid readability in body text contexts. It is particularly useful for educational and publishing projects serving Arabic-speaking audiences.

name: Headland One
classification_notes: Headland One features high contrast between thick and thin strokes with sharp, elegant serifs reminiscent of classical display typefaces. It carries a refined, editorial quality suited to headline use rather than extended text.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with sharp, classical proportions that commands attention at large sizes.
description: Headland One draws on the tradition of elegant display serifs, with pronounced thick-thin contrast and crisp serifs that make it well suited to editorial headlines, magazine covers, and branding. Its single weight limits versatility but concentrates its character effectively. It works best set large, where its refined details can be fully appreciated.

name: Hedvig Letters Sans
classification_notes: Hedvig Letters Sans is a contemporary humanist sans with broad language support including mathematical symbols, designed with clarity and neutrality as guiding principles. Its letterforms are open and even-spaced, prioritising readability across contexts.
summary: A contemporary humanist sans with extensive script and mathematical coverage, designed for clarity in academic and technical settings.
description: Hedvig Letters Sans offers broad character support, including Latin Extended and mathematical symbols, making it a practical choice for scientific publishing, educational platforms, and technical documentation. Its clean, open construction reads well at body sizes on screen and in print. The typeface pairs naturally with its serif sibling for coherent typographic systems.

name: Hedvig Letters Serif
classification_notes: Hedvig Letters Serif shares the design sensibility of its sans companion, offering open, readable letterforms with moderate contrast and broad language and mathematical support. It is understated and functional rather than characterful.
summary: A humanist serif companion to Hedvig Letters Sans, designed for readability in technical and academic contexts with full mathematical support.
description: Hedvig Letters Serif provides a warm, legible counterpart to the sans version, suited to body text in scientific papers, educational materials, and multi-script publications. Its moderate stroke contrast and open apertures ensure comfort over long reading sessions. Together with the sans, it enables a cohesive and functional typographic system.

name: Heebo
classification_notes: Heebo is a clean grotesque sans with Hebrew and Latin support, designed to harmonise both scripts at matching optical sizes and weights. Its neutral character and wide weight range make it highly versatile for UI and editorial use.
summary: A versatile grotesque sans spanning Hebrew and Latin, with a wide weight range and clean neutrality suited to digital interfaces.
description: Heebo is one of the most capable Hebrew-Latin typefaces available on Google Fonts, with nine weights enabling refined typographic hierarchy across both scripts. Its restrained, grotesque character makes it equally at home in mobile apps, web interfaces, and editorial layouts. It is a reliable workhorse for any project requiring Hebrew and Latin bilingual support.

name: Henny Penny
classification_notes: Henny Penny is a playful, hand-drawn display typeface with chunky, irregular letterforms that evoke children's illustration and folksy craft aesthetics. It has strong personality but very limited typographic range.
summary: A chunky, hand-drawn display face with a folksy, playful character suited to children's content and novelty branding.
description: Henny Penny's irregular, illustrated letterforms give it immediate personality, making it useful for children's books, food packaging, and informal event graphics. Its single weight and purely decorative nature mean it functions only as a headline or logotype element, never for body text. It is firmly a one-trick typeface, best used sparingly.

name: Hepta Slab
classification_notes: Hepta Slab is a variable slab serif with a striking range from hairline to ultra-black, featuring geometric construction and robust, blocky serifs that intensify dramatically at heavier weights. Its extreme weight contrast makes it particularly compelling for display use.
summary: A slab serif with a dramatic weight range from hairline to ultra-black, offering striking visual versatility through its variable axis.
description: Hepta Slab's nine static weights and variable font capability make it one of the more expressive slab serifs on Google Fonts, capable of moving from delicate editorial text to thunderous display headlines. Its geometric skeleton and sturdy serifs give it a confident, modern character. It suits branding, poster design, and editorial projects where typographic impact is essential.

name: Herr Von Muellerhoff
classification_notes: Herr Von Muellerhoff is a formal, copperplate-influenced script with elaborate swash capitals and flowing, connected letterforms reminiscent of nineteenth-century penmanship. Its ornate quality limits it to purely decorative headline use.
summary: An elaborate formal script evoking nineteenth-century copperplate penmanship, with flourishing capitals and flowing connections.
description: Herr Von Muellerhoff channels the ornate tradition of Victorian penmanship, making it a natural choice for wedding stationery, luxury branding, and period-themed poster work. Its single weight and highly decorative nature mean it should be used exclusively at large sizes for titles or short display text. Legibility at small sizes is poor, so restraint is essential.

name: Hi Melody
classification_notes: A casual, handwritten script with a soft, rounded feel designed to harmonise Korean Hangul and Latin letterforms. The strokes are loose and friendly, evoking personal handwriting rather than formal calligraphy.
summary: A soft, handwritten script that pairs Korean Hangul with Latin in a warmly casual style.
description: Hi Melody brings a lighthearted, personal quality to bilingual Korean–Latin typesetting, making it well suited to greeting cards, social media graphics, and youth-oriented branding. Its rounded, flowing strokes give it an approachable character without tipping into overly childish territory. Best reserved for short display text rather than extended reading.

name: Hina Mincho
classification_notes: A delicate, high-contrast Mincho-style typeface covering Japanese, Cyrillic, and Latin scripts with fine hairline serifs and elegant stroke modulation. The design prioritises refined aesthetics over robust legibility at small sizes.
summary: An elegant Mincho serif spanning Japanese, Cyrillic, and Latin with refined, high-contrast strokes.
description: Hina Mincho channels the classical tradition of Japanese Mincho typography, with thin hairlines and pronounced stroke contrast that give it a literary, understated elegance. It is best suited to editorial contexts, long-form Japanese reading, or multilingual documents where a consistent, cultured tone is required. Its fine strokes mean it is better used at medium-to-large sizes for optimal clarity.

name: Hind
classification_notes: A clean, humanist sans-serif designed for harmonious use across Devanagari and Latin scripts, with open apertures and moderate stroke contrast that aid legibility at text sizes. The Devanagari component was developed with careful attention to script conventions.
summary: A practical humanist sans designed for seamless bilingual Devanagari and Latin typesetting.
description: Hind was developed by the Indian Type Foundry as a workhorse text face that treats Devanagari and Latin as equal partners rather than afterthoughts. Its open, humanist forms make it highly legible on screen and in print, and its five-weight range covers everything from light body copy to bold headlines. It is a reliable choice for apps, websites, and documents requiring both scripts.

name: Hind Guntur
classification_notes: Part of the Hind superfamily, this variant provides matching Telugu and Latin scripts with consistent humanist proportions and open letterforms. The Telugu component respects the script's distinctive circular strokes while maintaining visual harmony with the Latin.
summary: A humanist sans from the Hind family, pairing Telugu and Latin in a cohesive, legible text face.
description: Hind Guntur extends the Hind superfamily's proven approach to bilingual type design into Telugu and Latin, offering five weights for flexible use across UI and editorial contexts. The design preserves the circular, curved character of Telugu script while aligning with the clean humanist structure of its Latin counterpart. It is a dependable choice for digital products and publications serving Telugu-speaking audiences.

name: Hind Madurai
classification_notes: The Tamil member of the Hind superfamily, designed to unify Tamil's distinctive curved script with a harmonious Latin companion across five weights. Stroke endings and proportions are calibrated for screen legibility.
summary: A well-considered bilingual sans pairing Tamil and Latin within the cohesive Hind superfamily.
description: Hind Madurai applies the Indian Type Foundry's rigorous multi-script methodology to Tamil, one of the world's oldest living scripts, creating a typeface that works equally well in both Latin and Tamil contexts. The five-weight range makes it adaptable for interfaces, editorial design, and wayfinding systems. Its open forms and consistent spacing ensure readability across a variety of screen sizes and resolutions.

name: Hind Mysuru
classification_notes: The Kannada-supporting member of the Hind superfamily, maintaining the family's humanist proportions while accommodating Kannada's complex akhand conjuncts and distinctive letterforms. Latin and Kannada weights are optically matched.
summary: A reliable bilingual humanist sans serving Kannada and Latin within the unified Hind family.
description: Hind Mysuru brings the Hind superfamily's consistent design language to Kannada, a script characterised by rounded, looped letterforms that demand careful spacing and stroke calibration. The result is a text face that holds up well in digital interfaces, newspaper layouts, and mobile applications serving Kannada-speaking users. Five weights provide enough range for clear typographic hierarchy in both scripts.

name: Hind Siliguri
classification_notes: The Bengali companion in the Hind superfamily, balancing the rounded, looping strokes of Bengali script with the clean humanist structure of the Latin. Designed for strong on-screen performance across all five weights.
summary: A dependable Bengali–Latin humanist sans that maintains the Hind family's high standards of legibility.
description: Hind Siliguri extends the Hind superfamily to Bengali, a widely spoken script with distinctive headline strokes (matra) and complex conjunct characters. The Indian Type Foundry's careful engineering ensures these features render cleanly at small sizes on screen, making it suitable for apps, websites, and digital publishing targeting Bengali-speaking audiences. The Latin component holds its own as a standalone workhorse text face.

name: Hind Vadodara
classification_notes: The Gujarati-supporting member of the Hind superfamily, designed to harmonise Gujarati's rounded, headline-free script with a well-proportioned humanist Latin. Consistent x-heights and stroke weights unify the two scripts visually.
summary: A coherent Gujarati–Latin humanist sans, rounding out the versatile Hind superfamily.
description: Hind Vadodara addresses the typographic needs of Gujarati, a script notable for its absence of the overline common to related Indic scripts, giving it a distinct visual rhythm that the designers have carefully balanced against the Latin. Like its siblings, it performs well in interfaces and editorial contexts requiring bilingual typesetting across five weights. It is a solid, unpretentious choice for any project demanding high-quality Gujarati and Latin text.

name: Holtwood One SC
classification_notes: A heavy small-caps serif with strong slab-like qualities and pronounced stroke contrast, designed for impactful single-weight display use. The letterforms are robust and compressed, lending a commanding presence at large sizes.
summary: A heavyweight small-caps serif built entirely for commanding headlines and display settings.
description: Holtwood One SC offers a single, bold small-caps weight that makes an immediate impression in titles, posters, and editorial headers. Its dense, high-contrast letterforms suit heritage branding and print-inspired digital work. The lack of a lowercase and limited weight range restricts its versatility, but within its intended role it performs confidently.

name: Homemade Apple
classification_notes: A loose, irregular handwritten script mimicking a hurried personal signature, with highly variable letterforms and extreme informality. Stroke weights are inconsistent and connections are rough, reinforcing the personal, unpolished aesthetic.
summary: A rough, casual handwriting script that evokes a genuine personal signature rather than polished calligraphy.
description: Homemade Apple leans into imperfection, with uneven baselines and irregular strokes that give it an authentically hand-crafted quality. It suits informal branding, greeting card designs, and contexts where warmth and personality matter more than legibility at small sizes. Best used sparingly at large display sizes where its expressive character can shine.

name: Homenaje
classification_notes: A narrow, single-weight grotesque sans-serif with a tall x-height and tight proportions suited to space-constrained display use. The design is straightforward and functional with minimal ornamentation.
summary: A no-frills condensed grotesque that packs a lot of text into a narrow column with minimal fuss.
description: Homenaje is a single-weight condensed sans-serif designed to maximise text density in headlines and posters. Its restrained, utilitarian character makes it workable for signage, newspaper-style headers, and tight layout constraints. The absence of multiple weights limits its typographic range, but its compact footprint is its core strength.

name: Honk
classification_notes: A highly experimental variable color font with a distinctive layered, almost liquid three-dimensional appearance achieved through multiple colour layers and variable axes. It represents a technically adventurous direction in display typography, blending retro neon aesthetics with modern font technology.
summary: A bold, visually exuberant colour variable font that brings a retro-neon three-dimensional energy to display settings.
description: Honk uses COLRv1 colour font technology to create layered, glowing letterforms that are striking at large sizes and in digital environments that support colour fonts. Its variable axes allow control over the chromatic depth, making it adaptable for animated or interactive applications. This is firmly a one-trick novelty font, but within that trick it is genuinely impressive and technically innovative.

name: Host Grotesk
classification_notes: A contemporary grotesque sans-serif with roots in the classic European grotesque tradition but with subtle humanist refinements that improve screen legibility. It offers a broad weight range and clean, balanced proportions that work well across both text and interface contexts.
summary: A well-balanced contemporary grotesque that bridges classic Swiss neutrality with the legibility demands of modern screen typography.
description: Host Grotesk is a versatile workhorse sans-serif that performs reliably across UI, body text, and headline applications. Its seven-weight range gives designers meaningful typographic control, and its Latin Extended coverage makes it a practical choice for multilingual European projects. Clean without being cold, it occupies the well-populated but always useful territory between Helvetica rigidity and full humanist warmth.

name: Hubballi
classification_notes: A humanist sans-serif designed to support the Kannada script alongside Latin, with open, legible letterforms that prioritise readability in text settings. The design balances script-specific optical requirements with a clean Latin companion.
summary: A multilingual humanist sans designed with genuine care for Kannada script alongside a clean Latin companion.
description: Hubballi is a functional, readable sans-serif that serves Kannada-language digital publishing as a primary design goal, with the Latin script treated as an equal partner rather than an afterthought. Its open letterforms and generous x-height make it suitable for body text in both scripts at screen sizes. The single-weight restriction limits its typographic versatility, but its multilingual commitment is commendable.

name: Hubot Sans
classification_notes: A wide-ranging grotesque sans-serif developed by GitHub with a broad weight spectrum and variable font support, designed for technical and interface contexts. It features slightly condensed proportions and strong performance in dense UI environments.
summary: GitHub's own grotesque sans, built for the demands of technical interfaces with a generous weight range and variable font support.
description: Hubot Sans was created by GitHub for use across their products and design systems, giving it strong credentials in technical and developer-facing UI contexts. Its variable font axis spans from ExtraLight to Black, providing fine-grained weight control that suits complex interface hierarchies. Vietnamese and Latin Extended support broadens its usability beyond purely English-language products.

name: Huninn
classification_notes: A Traditional Chinese sans-serif with Cyrillic and Latin support, designed for East Asian digital typography with consistent stroke weights and clean geometric construction. The multi-script coverage is extensive, prioritising Chinese Traditional characters as the primary design target.
summary: A Traditional Chinese sans-serif with unusually broad multi-script coverage spanning CJK, Cyrillic, and Latin in a single typeface.
description: Huninn is built around Traditional Chinese script requirements, with a clean, even-stroked sans-serif aesthetic that holds up well in digital body text and interface applications. The inclusion of Cyrillic alongside Latin makes it genuinely rare among CJK fonts and a practical option for multilingual East Asian publishing projects. Its single weight limits hierarchical range, but the breadth of script coverage is its defining strength.

name: Hurricane
classification_notes: Hurricane is a casual brush script with a loose, gestural quality and irregular baseline that mimics hand-lettering. Its thick strokes and natural ink texture give it an expressive, organic character.
summary: A loose, energetic brush script with a hand-lettered feel suited to bold headline use.
description: Hurricane captures the spontaneity of brush calligraphy with its irregular strokes and organic letterforms. It works well for short display copy, poster headlines, and branding projects where a casual, handmade aesthetic is desired. Its single weight limits versatility, but within that constraint it delivers confident personality.

name: IBM Plex Mono
classification_notes: IBM Plex Mono is a monospaced typeface with humanist touches that soften the mechanical rigidity typical of coding fonts. It balances technical precision with warmth through subtle stroke variation and open apertures.
summary: IBM's corporate monospace with humanist warmth, built for code and technical documentation across multiple scripts.
description: IBM Plex Mono is part of IBM's open-source type system and brings a distinctly human quality to the monospace genre, avoiding the cold uniformity of many coding fonts. Its wide weight range from Thin to Bold makes it adaptable across interfaces, terminal environments, and editorial contexts. Cyrillic and extended Latin support makes it a strong choice for multilingual technical projects.

name: IBM Plex Sans
classification_notes: IBM Plex Sans blends neo-grotesque structure with humanist details, reflecting IBM's engineering heritage while maintaining readability at small sizes. Subtle quirks in letterforms — such as the double-storey 'a' — distinguish it from purely neutral grotesques.
summary: IBM's flagship sans-serif balances corporate authority with humanist warmth across a broad weight range.
description: IBM Plex Sans is the centrepiece of IBM's open-source type system, designed to embody the company's rational, engineering-led identity without sacrificing legibility or personality. Its full weight range makes it suitable for everything from body copy and UI labels to bold headline statements. Greek, Cyrillic, and extended Latin support ensures broad multilingual deployment.

name: IBM Plex Sans Arabic
classification_notes: IBM Plex Sans Arabic extends the Plex design language into Arabic script, harmonising proportions and weight distribution with the Latin and Cyrillic counterparts. Its design respects Arabic calligraphic tradition while maintaining a contemporary, legible structure.
summary: A rare, well-executed Arabic extension of IBM Plex Sans that maintains design harmony across scripts.
description: IBM Plex Sans Arabic brings the visual logic of the Plex family into Arabic, offering a consistent typographic system for bilingual and multilingual interfaces. Its seven weights from Thin to Bold provide genuine flexibility for both display and text use in Arabic-language projects. The careful calibration between Arabic, Latin, and Cyrillic makes it an invaluable tool for global product design.

name: IBM Plex Sans Condensed
classification_notes: IBM Plex Sans Condensed applies the Plex design language to a narrow format, retaining the humanist warmth of the main family while maximising space efficiency. Letterforms are tightly set without compromising legibility.
summary: The condensed arm of IBM Plex Sans, offering space-efficient typesetting without sacrificing family cohesion.
description: IBM Plex Sans Condensed is a purposeful narrowed variant designed to work alongside the standard Plex Sans family in space-constrained layouts. It carries the same humanist grotesque character as its sibling, making it ideal for tables, data displays, navigation labels, and editorial headlines where column width is at a premium. The full seven-weight range ensures it can serve both text and display roles effectively.

name: IBM Plex Sans Devanagari
classification_notes: IBM Plex Sans Devanagari adapts the Plex design system to the Devanagari script, carefully balancing the complex structure of Devanagari letterforms with the clean, rational aesthetic of the wider family. Weight distribution and spacing are calibrated to harmonise with Latin text.
summary: IBM Plex extended to Devanagari, offering a coherent bilingual system for Hindi and other Devanagari-script languages.
description: IBM Plex Sans Devanagari is a thoughtfully constructed extension of the Plex system into one of the world's most widely used scripts, supporting Hindi, Marathi, Sanskrit, and other languages. Its design maintains visual harmony with Latin Plex across shared documents and mixed-script interfaces, making it particularly valuable for product localisation and multilingual publishing. The seven available weights provide genuine typographic range for both body and display applications.

name: IBM Plex Sans Hebrew
classification_notes: IBM Plex Sans Hebrew integrates Hebrew script into the Plex system with careful attention to the unique proportions and structural logic of Hebrew letterforms. The design achieves visual parity in weight and rhythm with Latin and Cyrillic counterparts.
summary: A meticulously matched Hebrew extension of IBM Plex Sans, designed for coherent bilingual typesetting.
description: IBM Plex Sans Hebrew brings the Plex family's neutral-yet-warm aesthetic to Hebrew script, supporting a full range of weights for flexible use in print and digital contexts. The right-to-left nature of Hebrew is handled with care, ensuring that bidirectional layouts remain visually balanced. This makes it an essential tool for product teams building interfaces or publications for Hebrew-speaking audiences alongside other Plex scripts.

name: IBM Plex Sans JP
classification_notes: IBM Plex Sans JP extends the Plex design philosophy into Japanese, harmonising kanji, hiragana, and katakana with Latin and Cyrillic counterparts through unified weight progression and proportional logic. It reflects a genuinely integrated approach to multi-script typography rather than a superficial adaptation.
summary: IBM Plex extended to Japanese, delivering a unified design system across one of the most complex script combinations in global typography.
description: IBM Plex Sans JP is a substantial undertaking that brings the Plex family's consistent design language to Japanese, covering thousands of kanji alongside hiragana and katakana. The result is a coherent typographic system for Japanese–Latin bilingual interfaces, publications, and software products that need reliable cross-script harmony. Its seven weights give designers meaningful control over typographic hierarchy in both Japanese and Latin text settings.

name: IBM Plex Sans KR
classification_notes: The Korean extension of IBM Plex Sans brings IBM's corporate humanist sans-serif to Hangul, with careful attention to stroke weight harmony across scripts. Designed to feel consistent with the Latin counterpart while respecting Korean typographic conventions.
summary: IBM's corporate humanist sans extended to Korean, delivering consistent cross-script typesetting for digital and technical work.
description: IBM Plex Sans KR brings the disciplined, open character of the Plex family to Hangul, making it a reliable choice for bilingual Korean-Latin interfaces and documentation. The weight range from Thin to Bold covers screen UI, editorial, and branding needs with equal competence. It pairs naturally with other IBM Plex family members for cohesive multinational typesetting.

name: IBM Plex Sans Thai
classification_notes: The Thai extension of IBM Plex Sans adapts the family's rational humanist sensibility to the Thai script, with stroke weights calibrated to harmonise with the Latin glyphs. Clean, open letterforms prioritise legibility at screen sizes.
summary: IBM's humanist sans brought to Thai, designed for seamless bilingual Thai-Latin typesetting across digital interfaces.
description: IBM Plex Sans Thai extends the Plex family's systematic approach to the Thai script, ensuring visual harmony when set alongside Latin text. It is particularly suited to software interfaces, technical documentation, and corporate communications requiring consistent multilingual presentation. The full seven-weight range makes it versatile across hierarchy levels.

name: IBM Plex Sans Thai Looped
classification_notes: IBM Plex Sans Thai Looped offers an alternative Thai letterform style using looped terminals, which is the more traditional form familiar to Thai readers compared to the loopless variant. Otherwise shares the rational humanist structure of the broader Plex family.
summary: The looped-terminal variant of IBM Plex Sans Thai, offering the more traditional Thai letterform style within IBM's systematic type family.
description: Where IBM Plex Sans Thai uses loopless forms, this sibling face restores the looped terminals conventional in Thai typography, catering to audiences who find the traditional forms more legible and familiar. The two variants allow designers to choose the level of modernity appropriate for their context while remaining within a single cohesive family. Both share identical Latin and Cyrillic Extended glyph sets.

name: IBM Plex Serif
classification_notes: IBM Plex Serif is a contemporary transitional serif with moderate contrast, sharp bracketed serifs, and the same rational clarity that runs through the Plex family. Designed to complement IBM Plex Sans in long-form and editorial contexts.
summary: A refined transitional serif designed as the editorial companion to IBM Plex Sans, with Cyrillic support and a full weight range.
description: IBM Plex Serif carries the Plex family's characteristic mix of rationalism and warmth into a serif structure rooted in transitional tradition, with clean bracketed serifs and moderate stroke contrast. It performs well in body text and editorial settings, whether in technical publications, annual reports, or long-form digital reading. Cyrillic coverage makes it useful across IBM's global communications.

name: IM Fell DW Pica
classification_notes: A faithful digital revival of typefaces from the collection of Dr John Fell, donated to Oxford University Press in the late 17th century, characterised by irregular ink-trap details, slightly uneven stroke widths, and an authentic letterpress texture. The face has a notably rough, organic quality that distinguishes it sharply from polished modern digitisations.
summary: A characterful digital revival of the 17th-century Fell types, preserving their irregular, letterpress-era texture with deliberate authenticity.
description: IM Fell DW Pica reproduces the idiosyncratic charm of the original Oxford Fell types, with slight irregularities in stroke weight and spacing that evoke hand-set metal type. It is best used at display or large text sizes where its personality reads clearly, in contexts such as editorial headings, book covers, or literary branding with a historical feel. The single Regular weight limits its versatility but reinforces its role as a speciality display and editorial face.

name: IM Fell DW Pica SC
classification_notes: The small caps variant of IM Fell DW Pica, incorporating drawn small capitals consistent with the Fell type tradition rather than algorithmically scaled capitals. Shares all the irregular, letterpress-era texture of its companion.
summary: The small caps companion to IM Fell DW Pica, providing authentic drawn small capitals for historically informed typesetting.
description: IM Fell DW Pica SC offers proper small capitals drawn to match the weight and character of the Fell types, rather than scaled versions of the full capitals. This makes it suitable for titles, running heads, and formal editorial layouts where small caps are a key typographic feature. Like its sibling, it is most at home in literary, historical, or editorial contexts that benefit from an aged, handcrafted quality.

name: IM Fell Double Pica
classification_notes: IM Fell Double Pica is a revival of a larger body size from the Fell collection, resulting in slightly more generous proportions and different texture compared to the Pica cut. The larger original size means subtler irregularities and a somewhat more composed appearance.
summary: A revival of the Double Pica cut from the Oxford Fell collection, with broader proportions and refined texture suited to display use.
description: The Double Pica cut of the Fell types was originally cast at a larger size, which translates to slightly more composed and spacious letterforms in this digital revival compared to the Pica version. It works particularly well for display headings, editorial titles, and literary branding where a period flavour is desired without excessive roughness. The single weight remains a constraint, but the face rewards confident, considered use.

name: IM Fell Double Pica SC
classification_notes: The small caps companion to IM Fell Double Pica, featuring drawn small capitals in keeping with the Double Pica's broader proportions and period character. Retains the same authentic, letterpress-informed texture as the rest of the Fell series.
summary: The small caps variant of IM Fell Double Pica, offering drawn small capitals that complete the Double Pica cut for formal historical typesetting.
description: IM Fell Double Pica SC provides genuine drawn small capitals sized for the Double Pica proportions, completing the typographic toolkit for editors and designers working with Fell types. It is well suited to chapter headings, pull quotes, and classical book layouts where small caps add distinction and period authenticity. As with all Fell faces, it is most effective when its historical character is treated as a deliberate design statement.

name: IM Fell English
classification_notes: A digital revival of a worn, historical typeface from the Fell collection at Oxford's Bodleian Library, featuring irregular strokes and an authentically aged character. The letterforms exhibit the rough, hand-pressed quality of 17th-century metal type.
summary: A deliberately rough and characterful revival of 17th-century metal type, full of historical imperfection.
description: IM Fell English draws from the historic Fell type collection donated to Oxford University, preserving the irregularities and wear of early printing. Its rough texture and uneven strokes make it ideal for period-appropriate editorial design, book covers, and projects demanding authentic historical atmosphere. It works best at display sizes where its idiosyncrasies can be appreciated rather than mistaken for error.

name: IM Fell English SC
classification_notes: The small caps variant of IM Fell English, featuring the same worn historical character but with small capitals replacing lowercase letterforms. Carries the same 17th-century metal type aesthetic with true small cap proportions.
summary: The small caps companion to IM Fell English, offering the same aged historical character in a caps-focused format.
description: IM Fell English SC provides true small capitals drawn to match the rough, period aesthetic of its companion face, useful for traditional typographic hierarchies in historically styled documents. The small caps lend themselves to titling lines, running heads, and pull quotes within editorial or book design contexts. As with all the Fell types, it performs best at larger sizes.

name: IM Fell French Canon
classification_notes: Part of the IM Fell series, this revival is based on French Canon type from the Bodleian's Fell collection, featuring larger, more commanding proportions than Fell English. The letterforms are generously sized with the same hallmark roughness and historical texture.
summary: A larger, more imposing member of the Fell revival family, based on historical French Canon type.
description: IM Fell French Canon is drawn from a larger type size in the original Fell collection, giving it broader, more emphatic proportions suited to headline and display use. The worn strokes and historical irregularities are even more pronounced at this scale, making it compelling for book covers, editorial headlines, and period-themed poster work. It pairs naturally with its small caps companion for typographic hierarchy.

name: IM Fell French Canon SC
classification_notes: The small caps variant of IM Fell French Canon, retaining the larger, more authoritative proportions of the French Canon cut alongside true small capital letterforms. Exhibits the same aged, imperfect character as the rest of the Fell series.
summary: The small caps companion to IM Fell French Canon, commanding and historically textured.
description: IM Fell French Canon SC delivers true small capitals in the larger, more stately proportions of the French Canon cut, suitable for classical typographic treatments in display contexts. It works well for titling, chapter headings, and decorative text where historical gravitas is required. Like all faces in the series, it carries a rough vitality that sets it apart from polished modern revivals.

name: IM Fell Great Primer
classification_notes: Based on the Great Primer cut from the Fell collection, this revival sits between the English and French Canon in terms of scale, with characteristic worn strokes and historical irregularity. The proportions are sturdy and well-suited to text and display settings alike.
summary: The Great Primer cut of the Fell revival series, balancing historical texture with versatile proportions.
description: IM Fell Great Primer draws from the Great Primer size in the Bodleian's Fell collection, offering proportions that sit comfortably between the more intimate English cut and the grand French Canon. Its worn, irregular strokes give it the same period authenticity as its siblings, making it useful across book design, editorial work, and historically themed branding. The mid-range scale makes it one of the more adaptable faces in the family.

name: IM Fell Great Primer SC
classification_notes: The small caps variant of IM Fell Great Primer, featuring true small capitals in the sturdy Great Primer proportions with the same aged, historically textured quality. Consistent with the design language of the entire Fell family.
summary: The small caps companion to IM Fell Great Primer, historically rich and typographically versatile.
description: IM Fell Great Primer SC provides true small caps to complement the Great Primer cut, enabling classical typographic hierarchies within the Fell family. Its proportions are suited to running heads, subheadings, and decorative text elements in editorial or book design. The inherent roughness of the letterforms adds warmth and authenticity that polished modern typefaces rarely achieve.

name: Iansui
classification_notes: A script typeface designed to mimic natural Chinese brush calligraphy, covering Traditional Chinese characters alongside Latin and extended Latin glyphs. The strokes have a fluid, hand-drawn quality consistent with East Asian calligraphic tradition.
summary: A brush-style script spanning Traditional Chinese and Latin, rooted in East Asian calligraphic tradition.
description: Iansui emulates the fluid, pressure-sensitive strokes of Chinese brush calligraphy, making it suitable for culturally specific display and editorial work across Traditional Chinese-language contexts. Its Latin characters follow the same calligraphic logic, ensuring reasonable visual harmony in multilingual settings. Best used at larger sizes for headings, cultural branding, and expressive display applications.

name: Ibarra Real Nova
classification_notes: A refined serif typeface with high contrast strokes and elegant proportions, drawing on the tradition of 18th-century book typography with a contemporary finish. The design features carefully balanced optical weights across its range from Regular to Bold.
summary: An elegant, high-contrast transitional serif with the poise of classical book typography and the refinement of contemporary design.
description: Ibarra Real Nova is based on Ibarra Real, a distinguished 18th-century Spanish royal typeface, redrawn and extended for modern use with a range of weights and italic styles. Its high contrast and well-proportioned letterforms make it highly capable for long-form text, literary publishing, and refined editorial work. The typeface carries an air of quiet authority that suits high-end branding and book design equally well.

name: Iceberg
classification_notes: Iceberg features tall, angular letterforms with sharp geometric serifs and a distinctly cold, crystalline aesthetic. The design draws on Art Deco influences with its rigid, stylised construction.
summary: A sharp, angular display face with a crystalline Art Deco character that commands attention at large sizes.
description: Iceberg's rigid, geometric construction and blade-like serifs give it a distinctive cold elegance suited to editorial headlines and fashion branding. Its single Regular weight limits versatility, but within display contexts it delivers strong visual impact. Best deployed at large sizes where its architectural detail can be fully appreciated.

name: Iceland
classification_notes: Iceland is a highly stylised geometric sans with a sci-fi, technological feel built from angular, modular strokes. Many letterforms are dramatically simplified or mirrored, giving the face a futuristic, almost alien quality.
summary: A boldly modular geometric sans with a stark sci-fi aesthetic built from angular, near-symmetrical letterforms.
description: Iceland's letterforms are stripped back to geometric essentials with a deliberate machine-like rigidity that suits technology-themed branding and speculative fiction contexts. Its single weight restricts use to headline and display applications. The face works best where an otherworldly or futuristic mood is the explicit goal.

name: Idiqlat
classification_notes: Idiqlat is a Latin and Syriac script typeface with refined, low-contrast humanist serif letterforms designed to harmonise across both writing systems. The relatively light weight range reflects its focus on scholarly and liturgical text settings.
summary: A carefully crafted bilingual serif designed to unite Latin and Syriac scripts in a coherent, readable whole.
description: Idiqlat addresses the genuine typographic challenge of setting Latin and Syriac text together, making it particularly valuable for academic, religious, or cultural publishing contexts. Its humanist serif structure ensures legibility across the limited weight range available. The font fills a real gap for anyone working with Syriac alongside Latin text.

name: Imbue
classification_notes: Imbue is a high-contrast transitional serif with a broad nine-weight range and extended language support, drawing on classical book typeface proportions. Its optical sizing characteristics and refined detailing make it well-suited to both text and display applications.
summary: A refined transitional serif spanning nine weights that balances classical elegance with genuine typographic utility.
description: Imbue's generous weight range and high contrast make it equally at home in running body text and editorial headlines, offering real flexibility for long-form publishing. Its extended support for Latin, Latin Extended, and Vietnamese broadens its appeal for multilingual projects. The careful optical calibration across weights elevates it above many free serif alternatives.

name: Imperial Script
classification_notes: Imperial Script is a flowing formal script with fluid, calligraphic letterforms reminiscent of copperplate engraving traditions. Generous swashes and high contrast between thick and thin strokes give it a ceremonial quality.
summary: An elegant formal script with copperplate-inspired letterforms suited to luxury branding and ceremonial applications.
description: Imperial Script evokes the refined tradition of engraved invitations and high-end stationery through its generous swashes and sharp contrast between strokes. Its single weight limits it to display and accent use, but within that role it delivers convincing period elegance. It works well for wedding stationery, luxury packaging, and fashion editorial contexts.

name: Imprima
classification_notes: Imprima is a clean, functional humanist sans-serif with even stroke weights and open apertures designed for comfortable screen and print readability. Its modest character gives it an unobtrusive, workmanlike quality.
summary: A modest, even-tempered humanist sans that prioritises readability without stylistic pretension.
description: Imprima's clean, open letterforms make it a practical choice for body text in digital interfaces and print documents where clarity is the primary concern. Its single Regular weight is a significant limitation, ruling out hierarchical typographic systems. Within its constraints, it serves as a reliable, unobtrusive workhorse for straightforward text settings.

name: Inclusive Sans
classification_notes: Inclusive Sans is specifically engineered for readability by users with dyslexia and visual impairments, featuring carefully differentiated letterforms and generous spacing. Its design draws on accessibility research to minimise character confusion while maintaining an approachable, friendly appearance.
summary: An accessibility-focused humanist sans designed from the ground up to reduce reading barriers for users with dyslexia and visual impairments.
description: Inclusive Sans takes a principled approach to letterform differentiation, ensuring characters such as l, I, and 1 are clearly distinct, which benefits all readers and not just those with specific needs. Its multi-weight range and broad language support make it a genuinely practical choice for public-facing digital products and educational materials. The design achieves accessibility without sacrificing visual comfort or typographic professionalism.

name: Inconsolata
classification_notes: Inconsolata is a humanist monospaced typeface with warm, carefully drawn letterforms that avoid the cold uniformity typical of coding fonts. Its broad weight range and attention to spacing make it one of the more elegant options in its category.
summary: One of the most refined humanist monospaced fonts available, bringing warmth and craft to coding and terminal environments.
description: Inconsolata's letterforms are drawn with a typographic sensibility rarely seen in monospace faces, making extended reading of code noticeably more comfortable. Its wide weight range from ExtraLight to Black gives it unusual flexibility for a coding font, supporting everything from terminal UIs to technical documentation. The extended language support including Vietnamese further broadens its practical appeal.

name: Inder
classification_notes: Inder is a single-weight humanist sans-serif with open apertures and a functional, unpretentious character. Its letterforms show subtle calligraphic influence, giving it warmth without sacrificing legibility.
summary: A modest, readable humanist sans-serif with open letterforms and a quietly warm character.
description: Inder sits comfortably in the space between utilitarian grotesque and warmer humanist designs, making it well-suited to body copy on screen. Its single Regular weight limits its range, but within that constraint it performs reliably for UI text, editorial paragraphs, and simple digital interfaces. It lacks the versatility of a full type family but handles modest typographic tasks with quiet competence.

name: Indie Flower
classification_notes: Indie Flower mimics informal handwritten lettering with bouncy, uneven baselines and rounded, playful strokes. It has the quality of a felt-tip pen drawing, with deliberate irregularity built into its forms.
summary: A bouncy, hand-drawn casual script that captures the charm of felt-tip handwriting.
description: Indie Flower is one of the most recognised casual script fonts on the web, beloved for its approachable, childlike energy. It works well for greeting cards, children's content, informal headers, and social media graphics where warmth and personality are priorities. Its deliberate irregularity means it reads as genuinely hand-drawn rather than mechanically perfect, which is precisely its appeal.

name: Ingrid Darling
classification_notes: Ingrid Darling is a flowing, high-contrast display script with elegant swash letterforms and generous connecting strokes. It leans into romantic and feminine calligraphic traditions with a contemporary sensibility.
summary: A romantic, flowing display script with elegant swashes and high calligraphic contrast.
description: Ingrid Darling draws on copperplate and contemporary calligraphic traditions to produce a typeface suited to wedding stationery, beauty branding, and editorial display work. Its swash letterforms and high stroke contrast give it a distinctly luxurious feel at large sizes. It is strictly a display face — legibility at small sizes is limited — but in headline and decorative contexts it delivers considerable elegance.

name: Inika
classification_notes: Inika is a compact humanist serif with moderate contrast and sturdy, readable letterforms designed to perform well at text sizes on screen. Its design draws on traditional book typeface conventions while remaining practical for digital use.
summary: A compact, sturdy humanist serif designed for reliable readability in digital body text.
description: Inika is a straightforward humanist serif that prioritises function over personality, making it a dependable choice for long-form web reading and simple editorial layouts. Its two-weight family — Regular and Bold — covers the basics without offering much typographic range for complex hierarchies. It performs best in modest, content-focused contexts where legibility is the primary concern.

name: Inknut Antiqua
classification_notes: Inknut Antiqua is a distinctive multi-script serif combining Latin and Devanagari in a harmonious old-style antiqua design with strong ink-trap details and a robust, textured character. Its wide weight range and dual-script support make it exceptional among Google Fonts offerings.
summary: A beautifully crafted multi-script antiqua serif uniting Latin and Devanagari in a rich, ink-trap-inflected design.
description: Inknut Antiqua stands out on Google Fonts for its genuine craft and thoughtful dual-script design, making it one of the more serious typographic choices available for Latin–Devanagari publications. The ink-trap details in its letterforms give it a slightly worn, presswork quality that suits editorial, literary, and cultural branding contexts admirably. Its broad weight range from Light to Black ensures it can carry full typographic hierarchies with confidence.

name: Inria Sans
classification_notes: Inria Sans is a refined humanist sans-serif with slightly quirky details and a warm, intellectual character born from the visual identity work of the French research institute INRIA. Its letterforms have a subtle calligraphic underpinning that distinguishes it from more neutral grotesques.
summary: A refined humanist sans with subtle quirks, designed for the French research institute INRIA.
description: Inria Sans carries a distinctive intellectual warmth that makes it well-suited to academic publishing, cultural institutions, and editorial contexts requiring a sans with more personality than a neutral grotesque. Its Light, Regular, and Bold weights cover essential needs for body and heading use, though the family is compact. Paired with its companion Inria Serif, it forms a coherent and characterful typographic system.

name: Inria Serif
classification_notes: Inria Serif is the companion serif to Inria Sans, sharing its warm and slightly idiosyncratic humanist sensibility with moderate contrast and well-crafted details. It reads comfortably at text sizes while retaining enough character for display use.
summary: A characterful humanist serif companion to Inria Sans, sharing its warm intellectual identity.
description: Inria Serif is a genuinely pleasing text serif with enough personality to distinguish itself from generic workhorse typefaces on the platform. It serves editorial, institutional, and long-form reading contexts well, and its harmony with Inria Sans makes the two fonts a cohesive pairing for complete typographic systems. The Light through Bold weight range is slim but sufficient for most editorial hierarchies.

name: Inspiration
classification_notes: Inspiration is a flowing, high-contrast formal script based on copperplate calligraphic traditions, with elegant connecting letterforms and refined swash details. It is designed strictly for display use, prioritising beauty over extended legibility.
summary: A flowing formal script rooted in copperplate tradition, built for elegant display applications.
description: Inspiration delivers the refined, luxurious quality of copperplate calligraphy in a digital format suitable for wedding stationery, luxury packaging, and premium brand identity work. Its high stroke contrast and elegant connecting forms create a convincingly handcrafted appearance at large sizes. Like all formal display scripts of this type, it should be used sparingly and at generous sizes where its calligraphic detail can be appreciated.

name: Instrument Sans
classification_notes: Instrument Sans features clean, rational letterforms with a geometric foundation tempered by subtle humanist details. It maintains strong legibility across weights while carrying a refined, contemporary character.
summary: A polished geometric sans with enough warmth to suit both editorial and interface work.
description: Instrument Sans strikes a confident balance between geometric rigour and approachable detail, making it versatile across branding, editorial layouts, and digital interfaces. Its range of weights from Regular to Bold gives designers sufficient flexibility without excess. It pairs naturally with its companion Instrument Serif for mixed-typography projects.

name: Instrument Serif
